## Template A: Checklist for **adding** a package

### Pre-submission Checks
- [x] Package requires building for `emscripten-wasm32` platform (not a noarch package), in other words, the package requires compilation.

<!-- If you have a noarch package, we suggest submitting your recipe to https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/ instead. -->

### Recipe Structure
Added `recipes/recipes_emscripten/[package-name]/recipe.yaml` with proper structure:
- [ ] `context` section with `version` (and optionally `name`)
- [ ] `package` section with name and version using Jinja2 templates
- [ ] `source` section with:
  - Source URL is valid and points to archive file (`.tar.gz`, `.tar.bz2`, `.tar.xz`, `.tgz`, or `.zip`)
  - Source URL contains `${{ version }}` template for version updates
  - SHA256 hash is correct (verified with `curl -sL <url> | sha256sum`)
  - Patches (if any) are included in `[package-name]/patches/` directory
- [ ] `build` section with appropriate script/method
  - Python packages: `${PYTHON} -m pip install . ${PIP_ARGS}`
  - R packages: `$R CMD INSTALL $R_ARGS .`
  - C++ packages: Uses `emcmake`/`emmake` or `emconfigure`/`emmake`
  - Rust packages: Uses `rust-nightly` and `maturin` or appropriate Rust build tool
  - Build number is 0
  - If the script is longer than 3 lines, a *build.sh* is included
- [ ] `requirements` section (build, host, run as needed)
- [ ] `tests` section
  - Python packages: `test_import_[package].py` file created and referenced
  - C++ packages: Test executable or package_contents test
  - R packages: Package contents test
- [ ] `about` section with license, homepage, summary

---

## Template B: Checklist for **updating** a package

- [ ] ⚠️ Bump build number if the version remains unchanged
- [ ] Or reset build number to 0 if updating the package to a newer version

---

## PR Formatting
- [ ] PR title follows format: `Add [package-name]` or `Update [package-name] to [version]`
- [ ] PR description includes:
  - Version being added/updated
  - Any special build considerations or patches applied

- **Version**: 

## Build Notes

items.title: Port Python core modules to C# NativeAOT
Feat/c python timeseries metadata
items.body:
## Summary

Adds C-wrapper support for listing devices and reading per-device timeseries metadata, with Python `TsFileReader` bindings. Device identity is a single `DeviceID` (path, table name, segments). Chunk statistics are exposed as a tagged layout: common header `TsFileStatisticBase` (`has_statistic`, `TSDataType type`, `row_count`, `start_time`, `end_time`) plus a `TimeseriesStatisticUnion` arm selected by `base.type`. Numeric arms expose `sum` (boolean sum of true values as `double`, integer sum as `double`, float/double sum as `double`), ranges, and first/last where applicable; STRING exposes lexicographic min/max and time-ordered first/last strings; TEXT exposes first/last strings only (no min/max).

### C API

**Types:** `TsFileStatisticBase`, `TsFileBoolStatistic`, `TsFileIntStatistic`, `TsFileFloatStatistic`, `TsFileStringStatistic`, `TsFileTextStatistic`, `TimeseriesStatisticUnion`, `TimeseriesStatistic`, `TimeseriesMetadata`, `DeviceID`, `DeviceTimeseriesMetadataEntry` (embeds `device` only), `DeviceTimeseriesMetadataMap`.

**Device listing:** `tsfile_reader_get_all_devices` → `DeviceID*`; free with `tsfile_free_device_id_array`. For a single struct, clear heap fields with `tsfile_device_id_free_contents`.

**Metadata:** `tsfile_reader_get_timeseries_metadata_all` (whole file); `tsfile_reader_get_timeseries_metadata_for_devices` (`length == 0` → empty map; `devices == NULL && length > 0` → invalid arg; when `length > 0`, each entry’s `path` is required). Free maps with `tsfile_free_device_timeseries_metadata_map` only (also frees nested device strings and statistic heap strings).

Read common statistic fields via `tsfile_statistic_base(&statistic)`; read `sum` and type-specific min/max/first/last via `statistic.u.bool_s`, `.int_s`, `.float_s`, `.string_s`, or `.text_s` according to `base.type`.

### Python

- `get_all_devices()` → `list[DeviceID]` (`tsfile.schema.DeviceID`: path, table name, segments).
- `get_timeseries_metadata(device_ids=None)` → `dict[str, DeviceTimeseriesMetadataGroup]`: `None` = all devices, `[]` = empty map, non-empty list filters by device path. Elements may be `DeviceID` (uses `.path`) or any object accepted as path via `str(...)`.

Statistic types map to `TimeseriesStatistic` plus subclasses `IntTimeseriesStatistic`, `FloatTimeseriesStatistic`, `BoolTimeseriesStatistic`, `StringTimeseriesStatistic`, and `TextTimeseriesStatistic` (with `sum` only on the numeric/boolean subclasses).

### Tests

- `cpp/test/cwrapper/cwrapper_metadata_test.cc`
- `python/tests/test_reader_metadata.py`

items.title: Update docs and python to C++ interface
Add links to C++ and Python reference docs
Replace Python bootstrap with minimal C++ scaffold
items.body:
### Motivation
- Move the project baseline from a Python bootstrap to a minimal, C++-first scaffold so runtime/tools assume a C++ project layout. 
- Remove Python runtime from the bootstrap/runtime path while keeping the repo minimal and avoiding added dependencies, downloads, CI, tests, or model code. 

### Description
- Deleted the Python bootstrap files and packaging (`pyproject.toml`, `src/local_tts/__init__.py`, `src/local_tts/cli.py`, `src/local_tts/paths.py`) and added a minimal CMake/C++ scaffold (`CMakeLists.txt`, `src/main.cpp`, `src/paths.h`, `src/paths.cpp`).
- Implemented a tiny C++ path API with `get_repo_root()`, `get_large_data_root()`, and `describe_paths_json()` that uses only the standard library and prints a JSON object containing `repo_root` and `large_data_root` via `describe_paths_json()`.
- Implemented deterministic large-data root resolution with precedence `runtime.local.json` (manual string extraction of `"large_data_root"`) → `LOCAL_TTS_LARGE_DATA_ROOT` environment variable → fallback `F:\\Local_TTS_Large_Data`, and ensured JSON output escapes backslashes correctly.
- Updated `run.ps1` to be Python-free and to run a CMake configure/build (preferring Release) and then execute the produced binary from common Windows build locations, updated `.gitignore` to keep `runtime.local.json` ignored and add minimal CMake/build ignores, and updated `README.md` to reflect the C++-first baseline while preserving `runtime.local.json.example`.

### Testing
- Configured the project with `cmake -S . -B build` which completed successfully. 
- Built the executable with `cmake --build build` which completed successfully. 
- Ran the produced executable `./build/local_tts` which printed a JSON object containing `repo_root` and `large_data_root` and exited successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_e_69dc826e3b28832b80ca800cd6818e24)

items.title: Proyecto TP2: puente Python, C y Assembly
items.body:
# Proyecto TP2: puente Python, C y Assembly

Este proyecto muestra un flujo completo de integración entre:

- Python 64-bit para consumir una API pública.
- Python 32-bit para actuar como puente con una biblioteca nativa.
- C y Assembly para procesar un arreglo de valores flotantes y devolver enteros.

La ejecución principal toma el índice de Gini de países desde la API del Banco Mundial, filtra los datos por código ISO3 y luego envía la lista resultante a la librería nativa a través de `msl.loadlib`.

## Contenido del proyecto

### `main.py`
Script principal de ejemplo. Hace tres cosas:

1. Consulta la API del Banco Mundial para el indicador `SI.POV.GINI`.
2. Filtra los registros del país elegido por código ISO3, por ejemplo `BRA`, `CHL` o `COL`.
3. Envía el arreglo de valores obtenidos al puente `ClienteBridge` y muestra el resultado devuelto por la librería nativa.

### `client.py`
Define `ClienteBridge`, una clase basada en `msl.loadlib.Client64`.

Su función es conectarse al servidor 32-bit y llamar al método remoto `bridge`, enviando una lista normal de Python y recibiendo una lista de enteros como respuesta.

### `server.py`
Define `Server`, una clase basada en `msl.loadlib.Server32`.

Este servidor carga `./libbridge.so` mediante `ctypes`, declara la firma de la función nativa `bridge` y transforma la lista recibida de Python en arreglos C antes de llamar a la librería compartida.

### `bridge.c`
Capa de puente en C entre Python y Assembly.

Recibe un tamaño, un arreglo de `float` y un arreglo de salida de `int`. Recorre el arreglo y llama a `convert(...)` para cada elemento, escribiendo el resultado en la posición correspondiente.

### `convert.asm`
Rutina en Assembly x86 de 32 bits.

La función `convert` toma un `float`, lo convierte a entero con la FPU y luego suma 1 al valor obtenido. Ese es el valor que termina regresando cada conversión.

### `asm_io.asm`
Biblioteca de utilidades de entrada y salida en Assembly.

Incluye rutinas auxiliares como lectura y escritura de enteros, caracteres, cadenas y volcados de registros, memoria, pila y coprocesador matemático. Es un archivo genérico de apoyo para prácticas de Assembly.

### `asm_io.inc`
Archivo de inclusión con macros para usar las rutinas de `asm_io.asm`.

Define macros como `dump_regs`, `dump_mem`, `dump_math` y `dump_stack` para invocar las rutinas de diagnóstico desde otros archivos Assembly.

### `cdecl.h`
Cabecera de compatibilidad para la convención de llamada C.

Define `PRE_CDECL` y `POST_CDECL` para que las declaraciones funcionen correctamente tanto con GCC como con otros compiladores C/C++.

### `build/`
Directorio con artefactos generados.

En el estado actual contiene:

- `build/bridge.o`
- `build/convert.o`
- `build/libbridge.so`

## Requisitos

- Python 3.
- Paquetes de Python: `requests` y `msl-loadlib`.
- NASM y un compilador C para Linux si quieres reconstruir la biblioteca nativa.
- Un entorno 32-bit compatible para el servidor de `msl.loadlib`, si tu instalación lo requiere.

## Forma de uso

### 1. Instalar dependencias de Python

```bash
pip install requests msl-loadlib
```

### 2. Asegurar que la biblioteca compartida esté donde la espera el servidor

`server.py` carga `./libbridge.so`, así que el archivo debe estar en la raíz del proyecto.

Si solo tienes la versión compilada dentro de `build/`, cópiala antes de ejecutar el ejemplo:

```bash
cp build/libbridge.so ./libbridge.so
```

### 3. Ejecutar el ejemplo principal

```bash
python main.py
```

### 4. Cambiar el país consultado

En `main.py`, modifica la variable `country`:

```python
country = "BRA"
```

Puedes reemplazar `BRA` por cualquier código ISO3 válido que tenga datos disponibles en la API.

## Flujo de ejecución

1. `main.py` descarga los datos del Banco Mundial.
2. `main.py` crea `ClienteBridge`.
3. `ClienteBridge` llama al método remoto `bridge` del servidor 32-bit.
4. `server.py` convierte la lista de Python en arreglos C.
5. `bridge.c` recorre el arreglo y llama a `convert` en Assembly.
6. `convert.asm` transforma cada `float` en un `int` y suma 1.
7. El servidor devuelve la lista de enteros al proceso principal.

## Resultado esperado

La consola muestra:

- El país consultado.
- La lista de valores del índice de Gini obtenidos desde la API.
- El arreglo devuelto por la librería nativa.
- Un aviso si la función C retorna un estado distinto de cero.

## Notas importantes

- El script principal usa la API pública del Banco Mundial, así que necesita conexión a Internet.
- El servidor está pensado para trabajar con `libbridge.so` en la misma carpeta donde se ejecuta `server.py`.
- Los archivos `asm_io.asm` y `asm_io.inc` son utilidades auxiliares; no forman parte directa del flujo de `main.py`, pero sí son parte del material presente en el proyecto.

items.title: [Python] Only use Python Limited C API in ROOT Pythonizations library
items.body:
This follows up on 4e0c7bf0dda25984c, swapping out the remaining use of CPython API functions that were not inside the limited API.

In the cases where we're sill using private headers from cppyy, the parts of these headers that don't compile with the limited API are excluded via the `Py_LIMITED_API` preprocessor macro.

items.title: Python code cleanup and C subset compiler
items.body:
## Summary

- **Python cleanup**: Add ruff linting/formatting config, expand all abbreviations to full words, enforce keyword-only arguments (sorted) on multi-param functions, positional-only on single-param instance methods, add docstrings, sort functions alphabetically within scope, sort collection literals.
- **C compiler** (`cc.py`): host-side Python compiler that translates a C subset to NASM-compatible assembly. Supports `void`/`int`/`char*` types, local variables, `while` and `if` with comparison conditions, `+`/`-`/`*`/`/`/`%` arithmetic, `sizeof(var)`/`sizeof(type)`, `char*[]` array declarations with brace initializers, array indexing, `+=` compound assignment, char literals, call expressions, and `puts`/`putc`/`print_dec`/`uptime` builtins.
- **Codegen optimizations**: register operands (direct CX load for constants/variables), division remainder tracking across DX-safe calls, transitive remainder reuse (`(A%N)%M == A%M` when `M|N`), frame elision for `main()` using static labels, dead store elimination, and peephole passes (store-reload elimination, `mov al` for putc constants, dead epilogue in main).
- **argc/argv**: `main(int argc, char *argv[])` emits startup code that splits `[EXEC_ARG]` at spaces into a word-aligned `_argv` array.
- **Programs**: `hello.c` (puts), `loop.c` (while + int), `loop_array.c` (char*[] + sizeof), `echo.c` (argc/argv + if), `uptime.c` (division, modulo, print_dec — compiles to 74 bytes, one byte smaller than hand-written assembly).
- **Build integration**: `make_os.sh` compiles `src/c/*.c` through `cc.py` then NASM automatically. `test_asm.py` compiles only C programs not already in `static/`.
- **Assembler**: `xchg ax, r16` now emits the 1-byte short form (`90h + reg`), matching NASM.
- **Filesystem**: `DIR_SECTORS` bumped from 2 to 3 (48 entries per directory). Tests updated to use `bin/` instead of `src/` for copy, move, and second-sector exercises.
- **Repo layout**: hand-written `hello.asm` and `uptime.asm` removed; C versions are now the source of truth.

## Test plan

- [x] `./test_asm.py` — all 20 programs self-host byte-identically
- [x] `./test_fs.py` — all 5 filesystem regression tests pass
- [x] `ruff check . && ruff format --check .` — no lint or format issues
- [ ] Manual QEMU: `echo hello world` prints arguments, `uptime` prints H:MM:SS

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Use getopts to receive the image name and options for `docker run`.

Add the `arm_c` language option and instructions to build an image.

items.title: Add Python conversion of Qt C++ TagLister
items.body:
Converts `TagLister.h` / `TagLister.cpp` (Qt C++) to idiomatic Python as `convert/TagLister.py`. TagLister scans raw XHTML source and produces an ordered flat list of `TagInfo` records — one per tag in document order — used for structural navigation of XHTML content.

## Key translation decisions

- **`TagInfo` / `AttInfo`** — C++ nested structs become `@dataclass` types
- **Qt string types** — `QString`, `QStringView`, `QStringList` all collapse to `str` / `list[str]`; `QStringView::isNull()` → `is None`
- **`QList` ops** — `<<` / `takeLast()` / `removeLast()` / `last()` → `append()` / `pop()` / `[-1]`
- **`parseAttribute`** — C++ took `AttInfo&` by reference; Python version returns a new `AttInfo` instead
- **Private methods** — C++ `private:` block becomes `_`-prefixed methods
- **Error output** — `qDebug()` → `print(..., file=sys.stderr)`

## Usage

```python
from convert.TagLister import TagLister

lister = TagLister(xhtml_source)
for i in range(lister.size()):
    ti = lister.at(i)
    print(ti.tname, ti.ttype, ti.pos, ti.tpath)

# Attribute extraction
ainfo = TagLister.parseAttribute('<div class="foo">', "class")
# ainfo.avalue == "foo"
```

items.title: Python packaging
items.body:
## What this PR does

This PR adds a complete Python packaging story to the project:

1. **A self-contained installable wheel** — `pip install` / `uv sync` builds and
   installs the `mlir_ktdp` Python bindings + all native libraries. No separate
   MLIR installation required at runtime.

2. **A pure-Python utilities package** (`tools_ktdp`) — ships alongside
   `mlir_ktdp` in the wheel. Fully live-editable: changes to Python files take
   effect immediately without rebuilding.

3. **A `ktdp-walk` CLI tool** — inspect any `.mlir` file's operation tree from
   the command line.

4. **A pytest suite** — three parametrized fixtures covering dialect
   registration, flat KTDP ops, and nested control flow.

---

## How it installs

The build backend is [scikit-build-core](https://scikit-build-core.readthedocs.io/),
which drives cmake under the hood. `uv` manages the virtual environment and
locks all dependencies.

```
uv sync --extra mlir
```

That single command:
- Creates/updates the venv
- Installs `mlir_wheel` (provides MLIR cmake config + Python support libs)
- Runs cmake → builds the C++ extension + CAPI library
- Installs the project in **editable** mode

---

## Frozen dependency versions (`uv.lock`)

All versions are pinned in `uv.lock` for reproducible installs:

| Package | Pinned version | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `mlir-wheel` | `20260409+3b3ac5a11` | MLIR cmake config + bundled Python support libs |
| `nanobind` | `2.12.0` | Python ↔ C++ binding library (runtime dep) |
| `pytest` | `9.0.3` | Test runner (`--extra test`) |
| `lit` | `18.1.8` | LIT test runner (`--extra test`) |

`mlir_wheel` is an **optional** dep — users with a custom MLIR build can pass
`CMAKE_ARGS="-DMLIR_DIR=..."` and skip it entirely.

---

## Wheel contents

The wheel is self-contained (~19 MB compressed, ~70 MB uncompressed). No
`mlir_wheel` needed at runtime.

```
ktir_mlir_frontend-0.1.0-cp312-cp312-linux_aarch64.whl
│
├── mlir_ktdp/                         ← cmake-managed
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── ir.py, passmanager.py, rewrite.py
│   ├── dialects/
│   │   ├── ktdp_nanobind.py           ← KTDP dialect Python bindings
│   │   ├── builtin.py, ext.py
│   │   └── _ktdp_ops_gen.py, _ods_common.py, ...
│   ├── extras/
│   │   └── meta.py, types.py
│   └── _mlir_libs/
│       ├── libKtdpPythonCAPI.so       34 MB  ← statically links all MLIR C API
│       ├── libMLIRPythonSupport.so     1.5 MB
│       ├── _mlir.cpython-312-*.so      0.3 MB ← core MLIR Python extension
│       ├── _ktdpDialectsNanobind-*.so  0.1 MB ← KTDP nanobind extension
│       └── libnanobind-mlir_ktdp.so   0.2 MB
│
└── tools_ktdp/                        ← scikit-build-core managed (pure Python)
    ├── __init__.py
    ├── __main__.py                    ← entry point for ktdp-walk
    └── ir_utils.py                   ← ktdp_context(), walk_module()
```

`libKtdpPythonCAPI.so` dominates size — it statically links all MLIR dialect
C API libraries so no other MLIR installation is needed at runtime.

---

## ktdp-walk demo

Parse any `.mlir` file and see a recursive, indented operation tree with result
types:

```
$ uv run ktdp-walk test/Ktdp/add-with-control-flow.mlir

builtin.module: []
  func.func: []
    arith.constant: [index]
    arith.constant: [index]
    arith.constant: [index]
    arith.constant: [index]
    ktdp.get_compute_tile_id: [index]
    arith.muli: [index]
    ktdp.construct_memory_view: [memref<96x64xf16>]
    ktdp.construct_memory_view: [memref<96x64xf16>]
    ktdp.construct_memory_view: [memref<96x64xf16>]
    scf.for: []
      ktdp.construct_access_tile: [!ktdp.access_tile<1x64xindex>]
      ktdp.construct_access_tile: [!ktdp.access_tile<1x64xindex>]
      ktdp.load: [tensor<1x64xf16>]
      ktdp.load: [tensor<1x64xf16>]
      tensor.empty: [tensor<1x64xf16>]
      linalg.add: [tensor<1x64xf16>]
        arith.addf: [f16]
        linalg.yield: []
      ktdp.construct_access_tile: [!ktdp.access_tile<1x64xindex>]
      ktdp.store: []
      scf.yield: []
    func.return: []
```

Indentation reflects nesting depth — `scf.for` body ops appear one level
deeper than the ops at the function's top level. Also usable as a library:

```python
from tools_ktdp import ktdp_context
from tools_ktdp.ir_utils import walk_module

with ktdp_context() as ctx:
    # ctx is an MLIR Context with all KTDP dialects registered
    ...

for op, depth in walk_module(source):
    print(f"{'  ' * depth}{op.name}")
```

---

## Tests

Four tests in `python/test/test_basic.py`, all passing:

```
$ uv run pytest python/test/ -v

platform linux -- Python 3.12.13, pytest-9.0.3
collected 4 items

test_basic.py::test_dialect_registration                PASSED
test_basic.py::test_walk[SIMPLE_MODULE]                 PASSED
test_basic.py::test_walk[KTDP_BASIC_MODULE]             PASSED
test_basic.py::test_walk[KTDP_CONTROL_FLOW_MODULE]      PASSED

4 passed in 0.02s
```

Each test fixture is an MLIR source string paired with an exact expected
`(op_name, depth)` list. A single parametrized `test_walk` asserts that
`walk_module` produces the complete expected traversal:

| Fixture | What it tests |
|---|---|
| `SIMPLE_MODULE` | Plain `arith` + `func` ops, flat structure |
| `KTDP_BASIC_MODULE` | `ktdp.construct_memory_view`, `ktdp.construct_access_tile`, `ktdp.load`, `ktdp.store` — all at depth 2 |
| `KTDP_CONTROL_FLOW_MODULE` | `scf.for` loop containing KTDP ops — verifies that `ktdp.load` and `ktdp.construct_access_tile` appear at depth 3, deeper than `scf.for` at depth 2 |

The depth check on the control flow fixture is the most important: it proves
the traversal is truly recursive and not just iterating the top-level ops.

---

## Developer workflows

### Recommended: `uv sync` with mlir_wheel

```bash
uv venv --python 3.12
uv sync --extra mlir --extra test   # build + install + test deps
uv run pytest python/test/          # run Python tests
uv run ktdp-walk path/to/file.mlir  # use the CLI tool
```

Edits to `tools_ktdp/` are live immediately. Edits to `mlir_ktdp/` or C++
require `uv sync` to rebuild.

### Custom MLIR build (no mlir_wheel)

```bash
uv venv --python 3.12
uv sync --no-install-project --extra test
CMAKE_ARGS="-DMLIR_DIR=/path/to/llvm-build/lib/cmake/mlir" uv pip install .
```

### Bare cmake — C++ only (no Python)

```bash
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja -DMLIR_DIR=/path/to/llvm-build/lib/cmake/mlir
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
```

Python bindings are **off by default** — the cmake option
`KTIR_ENABLE_PYTHON_BINDINGS` (default `OFF`) guards the entire Python setup
block. This prevents build failures on pure C++ environments where nanobind is
not installed.

### Bare cmake — with Python bindings

```bash
cmake -S . -B build -GNinja \
    -DMLIR_DIR=/path/to/llvm-build/lib/cmake/mlir \
    -DKTIR_ENABLE_PYTHON_BINDINGS=ON \
    -DPython3_EXECUTABLE=$(which python3) \
    -DLLVM_EXTERNAL_LIT=$(which lit)
cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
# then:
PYTHONPATH=build/python_packages/ktdp python ...
```

### LIT tests

```bash
cmake --build build --target check-ktir
# or directly:
uv run lit -sv build/test/Ktdp/    # must target build/, not test/
```

---

## Package architecture: why two packages?

`mlir_ktdp/` is entirely cmake-managed — cmake installs all its files
(generated Python, `.so` extensions, CAPI library) as part of the build.
Putting user-written Python in the same package breaks editable installs:
scikit-build-core's redirect mechanism only redirects files it discovers
itself, and it skips any file already installed by cmake.

`tools_ktdp/` is pure Python with no cmake involvement. scikit-build-core
manages it directly via `wheel.packages`, so editable installs redirect imports
to the source tree — edits are live with no rebuild.

items.title: Simplify Python calls from C++
items.body:
Creating `decimal.py` with the below code causes the standard library module `decimal` to be passed over for this file.

```python
import math


def is_nan(self):
    return math.isnan(self)


Decimal = type("Decimal", (float,), {"is_nan": is_nan})
```

When the `is_nan` method is removed or if it is updated to raise an exception, `SortedDict` rejects the key.

```python
In [1]: from pysorteddict import SortedDict

In [2]: d = SortedDict()

In [3]: d[Decimal(0)] = 0
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ValueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[3], line 1
────▶ 1 d[Decimal(0)] = 0

ValueError: got bad key 0.0 of type <class 'decimal.Decimal'>
```

Otherwise, everything works, as it should.

items.title: Switch to offline-first Jarvis architecture (Android + Python + C++)
items.body:
### Motivation
- Replace the prior web-based frontend/backend with an offline-first, multi-language architecture focused on an on-device APK entrypoint and local AI runtime named Jarvis.
- Keep offline operation as the default while allowing explicit, opt-in connectors to public REST APIs for live data when configured.
- Provide minimal scaffolds across mobile, orchestration, and core runtime layers to bootstrap development in Kotlin/Java, Python, and C++ respectively.

### Description
- Removed the previous JavaScript `frontend/` and `backend/` web app implementation and related Node artifacts to simplify repo scope. 
- Added an Android APK scaffold with `android-app/app/src/main/java/com/jarvis/offline/MainActivity.kt` as a Kotlin entrypoint for launching Jarvis on-device. 
- Added a Python orchestration brain at `ai-brain-python/jarvis_brain.py` implementing `QueryContext` and a `JarvisBrain` class that is offline-first and supports optional web API routing via `allow_web_api`. 
- Added a minimal C++ core runtime with `core-cpp/CMakeLists.txt` and `core-cpp/jarvis_core.cpp` to build a local `jarvis_core` executable, and added `docs/api-integrations.md` to document optional API integrations and integration rules; updated `README.md` to reflect the new direction.

### Testing
- Ran `python3 -m py_compile ai-brain-python/jarvis_brain.py`, which completed successfully. 
- Ran `cmake -S core-cpp -B core-cpp/build && cmake --build core-cpp/build`, which configured and built the `jarvis_core` executable successfully. 
- No other automated tests were present or executed in this change set.

items.title: Update to C++20 and update python schema
Harden engine.py, scope Flawfinder to C/C++ only, fix ADMIN_TOKEN workflow guidance
items.body:
## 📋 Description

12 open code scanning alerts on `engine.py` are **false positives** — Flawfinder (a C/C++ analyzer) was scanning a Python file, matching C function names like `getenv()` and `open()`. The `branch-protection.yml` workflow fails because `ADMIN_TOKEN` is configured as a profile PAT but not stored as a repository/environment secret.

**Changes:**

- **`flawfinder.yml`**: Replace static `FLAWFINDER_TARGETS` with dynamic `find`-based C/C++ file discovery. Python files are no longer in scope. Empty SARIF fallback when no C/C++ files exist.
- **`engine.py`**: Defense-in-depth hardening — `_env_securise()` validates env vars with regex + length limits (CWE-807/CWE-20), `_chemin_reel()` resolves symlinks via `os.path.realpath()` (CWE-362).
- **`branch-protection.yml`**: Replace generic error with step-by-step setup instructions including direct URLs to PAT creation, repo secrets, and environment configuration pages.
- **`tests/test_engine.py`**: 8 new tests covering validation acceptance, rejection (invalid patterns, oversized values), defaults, and symlink resolution.

```python
# Before — raw os.getenv(), no validation
token = os.getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN")

# After — validated with regex pattern + length cap
token = _env_securise("GITHUB_TOKEN")  # ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]{1,255}$
```

## 🔗 Issue liée


## 🧪 Type de changement
- [ ] 🐛 Correction de bug
- [ ] ✨ Nouvelle fonctionnalité
- [ ] 🔧 Refactoring (pas de changement fonctionnel)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation
- [x] 🔒 Sécurité / Protection
- [x] ⚙️ CI/CD / Outillage

## ✅ Checklist qualité
- [x] Mon code suit le style du projet
- [x] J'ai ajouté ou mis à jour les tests correspondants
- [x] Les tests locaux passent (`pytest tests/`)
- [ ] La vérification de type est propre (`mypy phi_complexity/`)
- [ ] L'audit phi passe (`python -m phi_complexity.cli check . --min-radiance 50`)
- [x] La documentation a été mise à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Aucun secret ni donnée sensible n'est inclus dans le diff

## 🖼️ Captures d'écran (si applicable)

Before — 12 false-positive alerts from Flawfinder scanning Python:

![Code scanning alerts](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11a69298-1f17-424f-9eef-23374c0fe415)

ADMIN_TOKEN failure in branch-protection workflow:

![ADMIN_TOKEN error](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d537fd2-263f-4cb4-b219-7934cfa0349a)

## 💬 Contexte supplémentaire

- Coverage at 85.58% (pre-existing, unrelated to this PR — threshold is 89%)
- The 12 alerts will auto-close once Flawfinder runs with the corrected scope on `main`
- `ADMIN_TOKEN` still requires manual configuration: create fine-grained PAT → store as repo secret → ensure `branch-protection` environment exists

items.title: [Python] Start switching ROOT pythonizations to Python Limited C API
items.body:
This patch replaces usage of CPython macros and non-stable APIs with functions compatible with the Python Limited C API (stable ABI).

Replace direct macros with API functions:

  * `PyBytes_AS_STRING`   : `PyBytes_AsString`
  * `PyBytes_GET_SIZE`    : `PyBytes_Size`
  * `PyTuple_GET_SIZE`    : `PyTuple_Size`
  * `PyTuple_SET_ITEM`    : `PyTuple_SetItem`
  * `PyList_GET_ITEM`     : `PyList_GetItem`
  * `PyList_GET_SIZE`     : `PyList_Size`
  * `PyInt_AS_LONG`       : `PyInt_AsLong`
  * `Py_DECREF`           : `Py_DecRef`
  * `Py_XDECREF`          : `Py_DecRef`

Furthermore, replace `PyUnicode_AsUTF8` with a helper based on the stable `PyUnicode_AsUTF8AndSize`. Also, replace `<Python.h>` with `"PythonLimitedAPI.h"`, where the `Py_LIMITED_API` macro is set.

This change is part of enabling PyROOT to build against the Python stable ABI.

items.title: C extension
items.body:
We bump the version to 1.8 (which would also include the change to docopt-ng).

In this PR, we remove the dependency to `pynacl`, which uses `cffi` and [forces the use of `bytes`](https://github.com/pyca/pynacl/blob/main/src/nacl/bindings/crypto_aead.py#L120-L275), rather than `bytearray`. That makes it difficult to use for big files, since every segment triggers lots of memory allocations instead of re-using the same buffer (see [pynacl issue#707](https://github.com/pyca/pynacl/issues/707)). Instead, we implemented a Python C extension that binds to libsodium (and tries to retain only the used functions in the final shared object).

We update the testsuite to run on macOS and ubuntu, for python versions 3.9 to 3.14. We adapt the compilation to a bundled libsodium, and to an already installed system-wide version.

We update the docs (showing on [readthedocs.org](https://crypt4gh.readthedocs.io/c-extension/)) to reflect the installation changes.

We did not push yet to PyPI (we will do so when the PR is merged, solving #52 at the same time).

**Efficiency**:
Here is a small benchmark: we encrypt a bigfile (10 GB) using version 1.7 and this new C-extension. We then decrypt the files using the version the other encrypted. We are using a Macbook Air 2025 (M4, 32 GB, with SSD).

We encrypt using version 1.7:
```
(v1.7) $ time crypt4gh encrypt --recipient_pk pubkey < bigfile > bigfile.old.c4gh                    
real  2m36.722s
user  2m34.142s
sys   0m2.200s
```

We encrypt using the C extension:
```
(v1.8) $ time crypt4gh encrypt --recipient_pk pubkey < bigfile > bigfile.c.c4gh                    
real  0m18.415s
user  0m14.699s
sys   0m1.968s
```


We decrypt using version 1.7:
```
(v1.7) $ time crypt4gh decrypt --sk seckey < bigfile.c.c4gh > bigfile.old.decrypted
real  2m36.260s
user  2m34.043s
sys   0m1.977s
```

We decrypt using the C extension:
```
(v1.8)$ time crypt4gh decrypt --sk seckey < bigfile.old.c4gh > bigfile.c.decrypted
real  0m28.526s
user  0m26.222s
sys   0m1.927s
```

Using `diff`, we get that `bigfile == bigfile.c.decrypted == bigfile.old.decrypted`

**Conclusion**: about 6 to 8 times faster ⇒ [Not too bad](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Kr0DIzqXkOQ)

items.title: Logging implementation xians-lib-python with C# parity
items.body:
### Summary
- Add full Python logging implementation aligned with `XiansAi.Lib/Xians.Lib/Logging`:
  - `xians.logging` package with `XiansLogger`, `ApiLogHandler`, `LoggingServices`, and `Log` model
  - context-aware metadata extraction from `XiansContext`
  - batch upload to `/api/agent/logs` with retry + flush-on-shutdown behavior
- Integrate logging lifecycle into `XiansPlatform` startup/shutdown and expose public API exports
- Add TRACE support (`logger.trace(...)`) and level mapping compatibility with C# semantics
- Improve robustness:
  - JSON-safe serialization for datetime log fields
  - event-loop-safe background uploader client usage
  - prevent self-logging recursion / transport log feedback loops
  - suppress noisy http transport logs on console while keeping app logs visible
- Add example and docs coverage:
  - web-search-agent logging usage + `/logtest`
  - custom-workflow-test-agent Logging Test Workflow/Activity
  - comprehensive `docs/LOGGING.md`

### Why
- Bring Python SDK logging behavior to functional parity with stable C# implementation
- Ensure server log ingestion is reliable under real workloads
- Provide clear usage patterns for app developers and validate via runnable examples

### Test Plan
- [ ] Run `examples/custom-workflow-test-agent/main.py` with:
  - `CONSOLE_LOG_LEVEL=INFO`
  - `SERVER_LOG_LEVEL=TRACE`
- [ ] Start `Logging Test Workflow` and verify:
  - trace/debug/info/warning/error/critical emitted
  - workflow completes successfully
  - logs appear in server dashboard/API
- [ ] Confirm no recurring idle upload feedback loop from `httpcore/httpx` debug chatter
- [ ] Verify no `datetime is not JSON serializable` upload failures
- [ ] Verify no `bound to a different event loop` errors during startup/definition upload
- [ ] Run `examples/web-search-agent/main.py`, trigger `/logtest`, and validate queue + server upload behavior

### Notes
- Console and server thresholds are now independent (C#-style behavior):
  - server can receive TRACE while console stays at INFO+
- Internal transport logger noise is intentionally filtered/suppressed for signal quality

items.title: Full-stack security audit: fix 33 vulnerabilities across Python, Rust, and C/C++ layers
items.body:
Comprehensive security audit across the entire Zexus interpreter codebase spanning three rounds. Identified and fixed 33 vulnerabilities in Python source, Rust core, C/C++ native extensions, shell scripts, and Node.js scripts. Each fix includes a corresponding exploit test payload.

## Round 1 — Python Interpreter Core (11 vulnerabilities)

- **Command injection** in `stdlib/os_module.py` — `shell=True` with unsanitized input → forced `shell=False` with `shlex.split`
- **Pickle deserialization RCE** in `stdlib/fuzz_module.py` — replaced `pickle.loads` with JSON
- **`eval()` injection** in `vm/jit.py` — replaced `eval()` with `_safe_parse_number` using `ast.literal_eval`
- **Path traversal** in `vm/filesystem.py` (VFS prefix collision bypass), `evaluator/evaluator.py`, `stdlib/module_manager.py`
- **Environment variable leakage** in `stdlib/os_module.py` — added sensitive key filtering
- **Tarball symlink escape** in `zpm/installer.py`

## Round 2 — Rust Core, Scripts & Remaining Python (16 vulnerabilities)

- **`scripts/zpm.py`** — `shell=True` command injection, unquoted shim variables, PYTHONPATH hijacking
- **`scripts/zx-luncher.py`** — TOCTOU/symlink race condition
- **`security.py`** — unvalidated file export paths allowing writes outside CWD
- **`stdlib/template.py`** — path traversal in `render_file()`
- **`vm/binary_bytecode.py`** — pickle serialization fallback → replaced with `repr()`
- **`evaluator/unified_execution.py`** — predictable temp file path → `tempfile.mkstemp`
- **`external_bridge.py`** — hardcoded placeholder crypto keys → `secrets.token_hex`
- **`testing/zpics.py`, `zpics_runtime.py`** — path traversal via unsanitized `test_name`
- **Rust core** — gas metering overflow (`checked_add`), bytecode deserialization without size limits, `unwrap()` panics in contract_vm/merkle/state_adapter, negative index cast in rust_vm

## Round 3 — C/C++ Native Extension Layer (6 vulnerabilities)

- **Arbitrary file read/write** in `native_runtime.cpp` and `cabi.c` — no path validation on `read()`/`write()`, allowing `/etc/passwd` or `../../` traversal

```c
// Before: accepts any path
PyObject *args = PyTuple_Pack(2, path, PyUnicode_FromString("r"));

// After: validates path, fixes memory leak
if (!zx_validate_path(path)) { Py_RETURN_NONE; }
PyObject *mode = PyUnicode_FromString("r");
PyObject *args = PyTuple_Pack(2, path, mode);
Py_DECREF(mode);
```

- **Unrestricted module import** — `PyImport_Import()` with no restrictions lets JIT code import `os`/`subprocess`/`ctypes` → added 21-module blocklist with submodule matching
- **Use-after-free in `atomic_add`** — `Py_DECREF(delta_val)` before `PyNumber_Add(current, delta_val)` → deferred decref via `delta_owned` pattern
- **Memory leaks (10 locations)** — inline `PyUnicode_FromString()` passed to `PyTuple_Pack`/`PyDict_SetItemString` never decref'd
- **Negative gas charge bypass** — negative amount increases remaining gas → added sign validation
- **Duplicate declarations in `cabi.h`** — removed

## C/C++ Module Use Cases

| Module | Role |
|---|---|
| `native_runtime.cpp` | C++ JIT function pointer symbols for LLVM backend |
| `cabi.c` | Portable C ABI bridge (Python-callable + JIT pointers) |
| `cabi.h` | Stable ABI header shared by C/C++ implementations |
| `fastops.pyx` | Cython-accelerated bytecode dispatch loop |

## Verification

- 19 exploit test payloads, 46/46 checks pass
- 115 existing tests pass, 0 regressions (1 pre-existing failure from missing `pycryptodome`)

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Fixes 33 security vulnerabilities across Python, Rust, and C/C++ layers to close RCE, path traversal, and sandbox bypass issues. Adds exploit tests and an audit report; all existing tests pass with no regressions.

- **Bug Fixes**
  - Python sandboxing: removed shell execution in `zpm run` and `os.execute`, filtered sensitive env vars, added AST-safe evaluation in debugger/accelerator/JIT, sanitized snapshot names, secured temp files, and blocked traversal in VFS, evaluator, module manager, templates, and ZPM installer.
  - JIT/bytecode: replaced `eval()`-based folding with strict numeric parsing and removed pickle-based constant serialization.
  - Rust core: prevented gas overflow with `checked_add`, added bytecode size limits, replaced `unwrap()` panics with errors, and hardened Merkle/state handling.
  - Native C/C++: validated paths for read/write, added import blocklist for dangerous modules, fixed a use-after-free and multiple leaks, and rejected negative gas charges.
  - Scripts: `postinstall.js` now sanitizes command names; `zx-luncher.py` resolves symlinks and only accepts `.zx` files.
  - Cryptography: `generate_sphincs_keypair` now returns runtime-random placeholders via `secrets`.

- **Migration**
  - `zpm run` and `os.execute` no longer run through a shell; scripts must be simple commands without shell operators.
  - File operations accept only in-project paths; absolute or traversal paths are rejected (templates, evaluator, VFS, audit export, installer).
  - JIT/native code cannot import dangerous Python modules (e.g., os, subprocess, ctypes); remove such imports.
  - Pickled constants are no longer supported in bytecode; rebuild artifacts that relied on pickle.

<sup>Written for commit 1aefd33e546f531fbc2c15608a7c919b0d13c890. Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

## Summary
This PR updates the development environment configuration to support modern Python versions and upgrades C/C++ language standards to more recent versions.

## Key Changes

### Python Version Updates
- Updated Python include paths from 3.6m-3.9m to 3.10-3.13 across build tasks and IntelliSense configurations
- Removed deprecated Python 2.7m support
- Updated global Python version from 3.9.6 to 3.12.9 in installation memo

- Upgraded C++ standard from c++14 to c++17

- Migrated from deprecated `python.pythonPath` to `python.defaultInterpreterPath`

items.title: feat: integrate unified message framing for C++ and Python
items.body:
## Summary
This PR completes the functional gap between C++ and Python by integrating the messaging framing system (LineFramer, PacketFramer) across all transport layers. It enables users to handle completely framed payloads instead of raw byte fragments, providing a consistent "message-based" experience in both C++ and Python.

## Changes
- **C++ Core Enhancements**:
  - Extended `ChannelInterface` and `ServerInterface` to support `set_framer()` and `on_message()`.
  - Implemented per-session framer management in `TcpServer` and `UdsServer` using a factory pattern.
  - Refactored `BuilderInterface` to store a `framer_factory_`, allowing builders to generate independent framer instances for multiple connections (critical for servers).
  - Resolved member shadowing and naming conflicts in server builders (renamed to `on_framed_message_`).
- **Python Bindings**:
  - Exposed `LineFramer` and `PacketFramer` classes to `unilink_py`.
  - Added Pythonic helper methods: `use_line_framer()` and `use_packet_framer()` to all transport classes.
  - Exposed `on_message()` callbacks which automatically handle GIL acquisition and byte conversion.
  - Updated Python documentation with comprehensive framing examples.
- **Test Stability**:
  - Improved robustness of flaky transport tests (`BackpressureReporting`, `MaxClientsLimit`, `StopSemantics`, `SendOversizedPacket`) by refining timing, polling, and data volume.
  - Fixed missing header issues in test files (`<thread>`, `test_utils.hpp`).

## Related Issues
- Fixes # (Functional gap between C++ and Python framing APIs)

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [x] Documentation update
- [x] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [x] Test coverage improvement

## Additional Notes
The framing system is now fully "Unified", allowing developers to switch between protocols (TCP, UDS, Serial, UDP) while maintaining consistent message-level processing in both supported languages.

items.title: Add comprehensive testing tools for C, C++, Qt, and Python
items.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a complete suite of testing and code analysis tools to the Docker image, enabling developers to write and run unit tests, measure code coverage, and detect memory issues across multiple programming languages.

## Key Changes

- **C/C++ Testing Framework**: Added Google Test/Mock (built as shared libraries), CppUnit, and Check for comprehensive unit testing capabilities
- **Memory Analysis**: Integrated Valgrind for detecting memory leaks and errors in C/C++ code
- **Code Coverage Tools**: Added lcov and gcovr for generating detailed coverage reports for C/C++ projects
- **Qt Testing**: Included QTest library (libqt5test5) for Qt-based unit testing
- **Python Testing Stack**: Added pytest, pytest-cov, pytest-xdist, coverage, and unittest-xml-reporting for comprehensive Python test automation and CI integration
- **Documentation**: Updated README with detailed testing tool reference and usage examples for each language, plus Claude configuration file with quick reference for developers

## Implementation Details

- Google Test libraries are built as shared objects (`libgtest.so`, `libgmock.so`) and installed to `/usr/local/lib` for easy CMake integration

items.title: fix(languages): enable TSX, C/C++, C# in binary; uniform language coverage
items.body:
## Summary

Four of the eleven advertised languages (TSX, C, C++, C#) were silently
disabled in the published MCP binary due to missing feature flags in
`crates/code-analyze-mcp/Cargo.toml`. The core library fully implemented
all 11 languages; the binary only shipped 7. Any user passing `.tsx`,
`.c`, `.cc`, `.cpp`, or `.cs` files received an `unsupported extension`
error from all four MCP tools despite the tool descriptions advertising
support for those languages.

This PR fixes the root cause and closes all related uniformity gaps
identified in the language audit.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `crates/code-analyze-mcp/Cargo.toml` | Add `lang-tsx`, `lang-cpp`, `lang-csharp` to `code-analyze-core` features list |
| `crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/javascript.rs` | Remove redundant per-item `#[cfg(feature = "lang-javascript")]` guards; add doc comment explaining `reference_query: None` rationale |
| `crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/csharp.rs` | Add `struct_declaration` and `enum_declaration` to `ELEMENT_QUERY` |
| `crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/typescript.rs` | Add named sub-captures (`@func_name`, `@class_name`, etc.) to all `ELEMENT_QUERY` node types; add unit tests |
| `crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/java.rs` | Add unit tests (element query + `extract_inheritance`) |
| `crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/python.rs` | Add unit tests (element query + `extract_inheritance`) |
| `crates/code-analyze-core/tests/integration_tests.rs` | Add integration tests for `.tsx`, `.c`, `.cpp`, and C# struct/enum extraction |
| `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` | Document JavaScript `reference_query: None` rationale; update language count to 11 |

## Test plan

- [x] 315 tests pass (was 303; +12 new tests)
- [x] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` clean
- [x] `cargo deny check advisories licenses` clean
- [x] GPG signed, DCO signed-off

## Notes

C/C++ integration tests assert struct/class extraction rather than function
extraction. This is a pre-existing parser limitation: `function_definition`
in C/C++ uses a `declarator` chain rather than a `name` field, so
`child_by_field_name("name")` returns `None` in the main parser path. The
tests are scoped to what actually works and document the limitation.

items.title: Move Python-defined tas to C++ implementation
items.body:
This pull request introduces support for several new Zero-Order Hold (ZOH) low-thrust dynamics models in both the C++ and Python APIs, along with improvements to documentation and build instructions. The main focus is on expanding the astrodynamics capabilities of the library, especially for low-thrust trajectory analysis.

### New ZOH Low-Thrust Dynamics Support

* Added new C++ header files and implementations for ZOH low-thrust dynamics:
  - [`zoh_kep.hpp`](diffhunk://#diff-e813404db9691af7f55332e434febdf255726c82827e627a94f3aa7719be6e65R1-R38): Keplerian low-thrust in Cartesian coordinates.
  - [`zoh_eq.hpp`](diffhunk://#diff-f5abf4df6d173bfa27a9e660bd69dc140e3fd1762f21536c09bad6600b0d4ee2R1-R38): Equinoctial low-thrust dynamics.
  - [`zoh_cr3bp.hpp`](diffhunk://#diff-50b8a5010a3b07659499dbfba42d8b1c93c908c2f7f6cf5f4a16ffa285cf40afR1-R38): Circular restricted three-body problem (CR3BP) low-thrust dynamics.
  - [`zoh_ss.hpp`](diffhunk://#diff-da29d2446fa946438e03d3bdc410e212fbed28cad7434044fbc24aa9b2324e19R1-R38): Solar sail dynamics.

### Documentation and Build Improvements

* Updated the `README.md` to clarify the relationship between `kep3` (C++ library) and `pykep` (Python package), emphasize the new version, and provide improved installation instructions using `ninja` and `cmake`. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L23-R45) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L52-R57) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L72-R78)
* Updated the Sphinx documentation configuration to reflect new copyright and authorship, crediting the Advanced Concepts Team, ESA.

### Testing

items.title: Add minimal Python sidecar and C++ localhost health bridge
items.body:
### Motivation
- Provide the smallest possible Python sidecar to establish the local HTTP boundary for future STT/vision/planner adapters.  
- Give the C++ shell a simple way to detect and log whether the sidecar is available so the app can degrade gracefully.  
- Keep the repo surface minimal (one `python/service.py` file) and avoid adding model or heavy infra yet.

### Description
- Add `python/service.py`, a single-file sidecar using the standard library `http.server` that exposes `GET /health` and `POST /plan` (mock planner response) and logs incoming requests.  
- Update `src/main.cpp` to perform a socket-based startup health check against `127.0.0.1:8080/health`, emit cross-language boundary logs, and continue running if the sidecar is unreachable.  
- Update `README.md` with concise build/run steps and manual commands to test the health and mock planner endpoints.

### Testing
- Built the C++ app with `cmake -S . -B build` and `cmake --build build`, which succeeded.  
- Launched the Python sidecar via `python3 python/service.py` and confirmed `curl http://127.0.0.1:8080/health` returned a JSON `{"status":"ok",...}`.  
- Sent a mock planner request with `curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8080/plan -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"recipe_id":"demo"}'` and received the mock `PlannerResponse` JSON.  
- Ran the C++ binary `./build/mimoca` and observed the health check succeed in logs and the app continue running; all automated checks passed.

items.title: Support RAII for C++ and ctx manager for Python
Fix Python detection to scan all drives instead of C:\ only
items.body:
Python detection was failing with ENOENT errors when installations existed on non-C drives (D:, E:, etc.). The existing logic only checked system PATH and hardcoded C:\ paths.

## Changes

- **New `detectPythonExecutable()` helper**: Centralizes detection logic with all-drive scanning
  - Enumerates all available drives (A-Z) on Windows
  - Checks multiple installation patterns per drive:
    - Microsoft Store (`WindowsApps`)
    - Python.org (`AppData\Local\Programs\Python`)
    - System-wide (`Program Files\Python`)
    - Direct installs (`D:\Python312`, `E:\Python3`, etc.)
    - Developer paths (`\bin`, `\tools`, `\dev`, `\opt`)
  - Standardized version regex: `/^Python3?\d*$/i` with configurable bounds (3.8-3.13)

- **Updated 5 Python-dependent handlers** to use new detection:
  - `runPytorchAutoInstall()` (first-launch auto-setup)
  - `install-pytorch`
  - `check-pytorch`
  - `reinstall-pytorch-cpu-only`
  - `fine-tune-start`

- **Enhanced error messages**: Detailed diagnostics showing drives scanned, paths tested, and actionable installation instructions

## Example

Before: Generic ENOENT errors with no drive context
```
Debug: tried python (spawn python ENOENT), python3 (spawn python3 ENOENT)
```

After: Full scan report with troubleshooting
```
Detection Summary:
  python: ✗ spawn python ENOENT
  Available drives: C, D, E
  Tested 3 installations on D:, none valid
  
Installation options:
1. Download from python.org
2. Install via Microsoft Store
3. Install via winget
```

items.title: Fix C++ vehicle update order to match Python
items.body:
## Summary
- Fix non-deterministic vehicle update loop to iterate in ID order (matching Python) instead of using `active_vehicles` with swap-and-pop ordering
- Fix hardcoded `noise=0.01` in `update_adj_time_matrix` to use the configurable `route_choice_uncertainty` parameter

## Background

The C++ `test_diverge_lesssaturated_2_1_1` test was failing ~75% of the time (vs ~25% for the equivalent Python test). Investigation revealed that the `active_vehicles` swap-and-pop iteration order in the vehicle update loop caused subtle interactions with the transfer shuffle at diverge nodes, systematically increasing upstream link density by ~10%.

In deterministic mode, Python and C++ produce identical results, confirming the core logic is equivalent. The issue only appeared in random mode due to the vehicle update ordering difference.

## Changes

**`uxsim/trafficpp/traffi.cpp`** (2 changes):

1. **Vehicle update loop** (main fix): Changed the non-deterministic vehicle update loop from `active_vehicles` (swap-and-pop, unstable order) to `vehicles` (ID order, matching Python's `VEHICLES_LIVING` iteration).

2. **Route search noise**: Changed hardcoded `noise = 0.01` to `noise = route_choice_uncertainty`, so the user-configurable `duo_noise` parameter is properly respected.

## Test plan
- [x] `test_diverge_lesssaturated_2_1_1` fail rate: 75% → 29% (Python baseline: 22%)
- [x] All 172 C++ mode tests pass (`--reruns 5`)
- [x] Deterministic mode produces identical results to Python
- [x] k1 mean: 0.082 → 0.076 (Python: 0.075)

Part of https://github.com/toruseo/UXsim/issues/297

items.title: chore: normalize C# source formatting
items.body:
### Motivation
- Normalize C# sources across the Unity project to make diffs and code reviews easier by enforcing consistent line endings and trimming trailing whitespace. 
- Apply a mechanical, non-behavior change cleanup to gameplay, editor, and bundled utility/reporter scripts. 

### Description
- Standardized line endings to LF, removed trailing whitespace, and ensured a single newline at EOF for C# files across the repo, affecting 22 `.cs` files including gameplay scripts, editor helpers and the bundled reporter utilities. 
- Changes are format-only and do not modify runtime logic or public APIs. 
- Normalization was implemented with a small Python normalization script that reads each `.cs` file, collapses CRLF/CR to `\n`, trims trailing spaces, and writes files back in UTF-8. 
- Files touched include `Assets/Script/*`, `Assets/GoogleMobileAds/Editor/Utils.cs`, and `Assets/Unity-Logs-Viewer/Reporter/*` among others. 

### Testing
- Ran a Python verification script (`python - <<'PY' ... PY`) that scans all `.cs` files for trailing spaces and reports `ok`, confirming no trailing whitespace remains. 
- Performed repository status checks to confirm only formatting changes were made and no behavioral files were altered by content (format-only validation succeeded).

items.title: [python] Bump Python to 3.14.4
items.body:
## 0. Summary of Changes

**Release date:** April 7, 2026
**Scope:** ~337 bugfixes, build improvements, and documentation changes

## 1. Security

| CVE | Issue | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| CVE-2026-4224 | [gh-145986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145986) | Fix crash from unbounded C recursion in `xml.parsers.expat` when converting deeply nested XML content models with `ElementDeclHandler` |
| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
| — | [gh-144370](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144370) | Disallow control characters in status in `wsgiref.handlers` to prevent HTTP header injection |
| — | [gh-143930](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143930) | Reject leading dashes in URLs passed to `webbrowser.open()` |

## 2. Core and Builtins

### 2-1. Crash / Memory Safety Fixes

- **[gh-148157](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148157):** Fix crash when parsing invalid type comments for function parameters (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146615):** Fix crash in `__get__` for `METH_METHOD` descriptors with invalid second argument
- **[gh-145783](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145783):** Fix unlikely parser crash when errors were not propagated (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145792](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145792):** Fix out-of-bounds access invoking faulthandler without VLA support
- **[gh-145701](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145701):** Fix `SystemError` when `__classdict__` or `__conditional_annotations__` is in a class-scope inlined comprehension (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145335):** Fix crash in `os.pathconf` when called with `-1`
- **[gh-145234](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145234):** Fix `SystemError` in parser when encoding cookie decodes to carriage returns
- **[gh-145142](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145142):** Fix crash in free-threaded build when `str.maketrans` dict is concurrently modified
- **[gh-144872](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144872):** Fix heap buffer overflow in the parser (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-144766](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144766):** Fix crash in fork child process when perf support is enabled
- **[gh-144759](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144759):** Fix undefined behavior in lexer with `NULL` pointer arithmetic
- **[gh-144601](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144601):** Fix crash when importing a module whose `PyInit` function raises an exception from a subinterpreter
- **[gh-143636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143636):** Fix crash when calling `SimpleNamespace.__replace__()` on non-namespace instances
- **[gh-140594](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140594):** Fix out-of-bounds read when a single NUL character is read from stdin
- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** Fix `repr()` for lists and tuples containing `NULL`s

### 2-2. Free-Threaded Build Improvements

- **[gh-148144](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148144):** Initialize `_PyInterpreterFrame.visited` when copying frames so incremental GC doesn't read uninitialized bytes
- **[gh-146041](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146041):** Fix scaling bottleneck in `sys.intern` and `PyObject_SetAttr` by avoiding interpreter-wide lock
- **[gh-145779](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145779):** Improve scaling of `classmethod` and `staticmethod` calls
- **[gh-145685](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145685):** Improve scaling of type attribute lookups by avoiding contention on internal type lock
- **[gh-145713](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145713):** Make `bytearray.resize` thread-safe using critical sections
- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects
- **[gh-145036](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145036):** Fix race condition in `list.__sizeof__`
- **[gh-144513](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144513):** Fix potential deadlock using critical sections during stop-the-world pauses
- **[gh-144446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144446):** Fix data races when reading frame object attributes while another thread executes the frame
- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code
- **[gh-146250](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146250):** Fix memory leak in `SyntaxError` when re-initializing
- **[gh-146245](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146245):** Fix reference leaks in `socket` when audit hooks raise exceptions
- **[gh-146196](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146196):** Fix potential UB in `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteASCII` by adding zero-length check
- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios
- **[gh-142183](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142183):** Avoid pathological slowdown from repeated calls at a specific stack depth
- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block
- **[gh-144563](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144563):** Fix Tachyon profiler interaction with `ctypes` and modules loading Python shared library
- **[gh-130555](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130555):** Fix use-after-free in `dict.clear` with embedded values and re-entrant mutation
- **[gh-130327](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130327):** Fix erroneous clearing of `__dict__` if overwritten at runtime
- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure
- **[gh-144833](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144833):** Fix use-after-free in `ssl` when `SSL_new()` returns `NULL`
- **[gh-143637](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143637):** Fix crash in `socket.sendmsg()` from re-entrant ancillary data mutation
- **[gh-143543](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143543):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` from re-entrant `__eq__` during key comparison
- **[gh-140652](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140652):** Fix crash in `_interpchannels.list_all` after closing a channel
- **[gh-142781](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142781):** Fix `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo` crash when internal class-level cache is inconsistent
- **[gh-142787](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142787):** Fix `sqlite3` assertion failure in blob subscript with empty slices

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

- **[gh-145883](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145883):** Fix heap buffer overflow reads from malformed TZif data in `zoneinfo` (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146310](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146310):** `ensurepip` no longer looks for `pip-*.whl` in the current directory
- **[gh-146083](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146083):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.5
- **[gh-144363](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144363):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.4

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-144777](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144777):** Fix data races in `io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder`
- **[gh-144809](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144809):** Make `collections.deque` copy atomic
- **[gh-144494](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144494):** Fix performance regression in `asyncio.all_tasks`
- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-146556](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146556):** Fix `annotationlib.get_annotations` hanging on circular `__wrapped__` chains
- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`
- **[gh-146076](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146076):** Fix `zoneinfo` crashes when deleting `_weak_cache` from a subclass
- **[gh-146054](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146054):** Limit size of `encodings.search_function` cache (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146004](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146004):** All `-X` options now propagated to child processes spawned by `multiprocessing`
- **[gh-145754](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145754):** Fix mock autospec with `FORWARDREF` annotation format
- **[gh-145750](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145750):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format string parsing (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145492](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145492):** Fix infinite recursion in `collections.defaultdict.__repr__` when self-referencing
- **[gh-145616](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145616):** Detect Android sysconfig ABI correctly on 32-bit ARM
- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)
- **[gh-145158](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145158):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format strings
- **[gh-144986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144986):** Fix memory leak in `atexit.register()`
- **[gh-88091](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/88091):** Fix `unicodedata.decomposition` for Hangul characters
- **[gh-144782](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144782):** Fix `argparse.ArgumentParser` to be pickleable
- **[gh-144259](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144259):** Fix inconsistent display of long multiline pasted content in the REPL
- **[gh-144156](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144156):** Fix email header folding with RFC 2047 encoded words
- **[gh-66305](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/66305):** Fix hang on Windows in `tempfile` when directory is non-writable
- **[gh-140814](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140814):** `multiprocessing.freeze_support()` no longer sets default start method as side effect
- **[gh-144475](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144475):** Make `repr()` on `functools.partial` safer during concurrent attribute replacement
- **[gh-144538](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144538):** Bump bundled pip in `ensurepip` to 26.0.1
- **[gh-144316](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144316):** Fix crash in `_remote_debugging`
- **[gh-143698](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143698):** Allow `scheduler` and `setpgroup` to be `None` in `os.posix_spawn`; raise `TypeError` instead of `SystemError`
- **[gh-142516](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142516):** Fix `ssl.SSLContext` reference leaks
- **[gh-143304](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143304):** Fix `ctypes.CDLL` to honor the `handle` parameter on POSIX
- **[gh-142763](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142763):** Fix race condition between `ZoneInfo` creation and `clear_cache()`
- **[gh-142352](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142352):** Fix `asyncio.StreamWriter.start_tls` data loss when upgrading to TLS mid-stream
- **[gh-141707](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141707):** Don't change `TarInfo` type from `AREGTYPE` to `DIRTYPE` when parsing GNU long name headers
- **[gh-139933](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139933):** Improve `AttributeError` suggestions for classes with custom `__dir__`
- **[gh-137335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137335):** Eliminate name conflicts for named pipes in `multiprocessing` and `asyncio` on Windows
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** Support lookup for Tangut Ideographs in `unicodedata`

## 4. Documentation

- **[gh-126676](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/126676):** Expand `argparse` documentation for `type=bool` with demonstration of surprising behavior
- **[gh-145649](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145649):** Fix text wrapping in `-X` option descriptions in the `python(1)` man page
- **[gh-145450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145450):** Document missing public `wave.Wave_write` getter methods
- **[gh-136246](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/136246):** New "Improve this page" link in docs sidebar

## 5. Build

- **[gh-146541](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146541):** Android testbed can now be built for 32-bit ARM and x86 targets
- **[gh-146498](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146498):** iOS XCframework build: ensure libpython isn't included in installed app content
- **[gh-146450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146450):** Android build script parity improvements
- **[gh-146446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146446):** iOS XCframework clean target is now more selective for single architectures
- **[gh-145801](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145801):** Use `-fprofile-update=atomic` with GCC PGO to prevent `.gcda` corruption

## 6. Windows

- **[gh-145307](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145307):** Defer loading of `psapi.dll` until used by `ctypes.util.dllist`
- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update bundled OpenSSL to 3.0.19
- **[gh-140131](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140131):** Fix REPL cursor position when module completion suggestion hits console width

## 7. macOS

- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update macOS installer to use OpenSSL 3.0.19
- **[gh-137586](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137586):** Invoke `osascript` with absolute path in `webbrowser` and `turtledemo`

## 8. C API

- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteRepr` now supports `NULL` argument
- **[gh-145010](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145010):** Use GCC dialect alternatives for inline assembly in `object.h` for `-masm=intel` compatibility
- **[gh-144981](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144981):** Make `PyUnstable_Code_SetExtra`, `PyUnstable_Code_GetExtra`, and `PyUnstable_Eval_RequestCodeExtraIndex` thread-safe on free-threaded builds

## 9. Tests

- **[gh-144418](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144418):** Android testbed emulator RAM increased from 2 GB to 4 GB
- **[gh-146202](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146202):** Fix race condition in regrtest temp directory creation on Windows
- **[gh-144739](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144739):** Skip `MemoryProtectionTest` when compiled with older expat but tests run with newer

*Source: [Python 3.14.4 Release](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3144/) · [Full Changelog](https://docs.python.org/release/3.14.4/whatsnew/changelog.html)*

items.title: fix: correct Python-C++ param name mismatches in bridge.py
items.body:
## Summary
- Fix 4 parameter name mismatches where Python bridge.py sent different keys than C++ plugin expected: `create_material`, `create_colored_material` ("name" → "material_name"), `add_canvas_panel` ("canvas_name" → "canvas_panel_name"), `reparent_widgets` ("target_parent" → "target_container_name")
- Add `TestParamNameMatching` test class (5 tests) to prevent regressions
- Fix 2 existing tests that asserted the old (wrong) param names

## Context
Discovered during Card Showcase demo when these methods were exercised end-to-end for the first time. None had existing integration tests.

## Test plan
- [x] All 111 unit tests pass
- [x] `create_colored_material` verified working against live UE editor
- [x] `add_canvas_panel` + `reparent_widgets` verified working against live UE editor

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
