- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects
- **[gh-145036](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145036):** Fix race condition in `list.__sizeof__`
- **[gh-144513](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144513):** Fix potential deadlock using critical sections during stop-the-world pauses
- **[gh-144446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144446):** Fix data races when reading frame object attributes while another thread executes the frame
- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code
- **[gh-146250](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146250):** Fix memory leak in `SyntaxError` when re-initializing
- **[gh-146245](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146245):** Fix reference leaks in `socket` when audit hooks raise exceptions
- **[gh-146196](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146196):** Fix potential UB in `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteASCII` by adding zero-length check
- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios
- **[gh-142183](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142183):** Avoid pathological slowdown from repeated calls at a specific stack depth
- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block
- **[gh-144563](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144563):** Fix Tachyon profiler interaction with `ctypes` and modules loading Python shared library
- **[gh-130555](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130555):** Fix use-after-free in `dict.clear` with embedded values and re-entrant mutation
- **[gh-130327](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130327):** Fix erroneous clearing of `__dict__` if overwritten at runtime
- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure
- **[gh-144833](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144833):** Fix use-after-free in `ssl` when `SSL_new()` returns `NULL`
- **[gh-143637](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143637):** Fix crash in `socket.sendmsg()` from re-entrant ancillary data mutation
- **[gh-143543](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143543):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` from re-entrant `__eq__` during key comparison
- **[gh-140652](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140652):** Fix crash in `_interpchannels.list_all` after closing a channel
- **[gh-142781](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142781):** Fix `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo` crash when internal class-level cache is inconsistent
- **[gh-142787](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142787):** Fix `sqlite3` assertion failure in blob subscript with empty slices

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

- **[gh-145883](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145883):** Fix heap buffer overflow reads from malformed TZif data in `zoneinfo` (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146310](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146310):** `ensurepip` no longer looks for `pip-*.whl` in the current directory
- **[gh-146083](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146083):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.5
- **[gh-144363](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144363):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.4

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-144777](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144777):** Fix data races in `io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder`
- **[gh-144809](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144809):** Make `collections.deque` copy atomic
- **[gh-144494](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144494):** Fix performance regression in `asyncio.all_tasks`
- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-146556](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146556):** Fix `annotationlib.get_annotations` hanging on circular `__wrapped__` chains
- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`
- **[gh-146076](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146076):** Fix `zoneinfo` crashes when deleting `_weak_cache` from a subclass
- **[gh-146054](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146054):** Limit size of `encodings.search_function` cache (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146004](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146004):** All `-X` options now propagated to child processes spawned by `multiprocessing`
- **[gh-145754](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145754):** Fix mock autospec with `FORWARDREF` annotation format
- **[gh-145750](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145750):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format string parsing (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145492](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145492):** Fix infinite recursion in `collections.defaultdict.__repr__` when self-referencing
- **[gh-145616](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145616):** Detect Android sysconfig ABI correctly on 32-bit ARM
- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)
- **[gh-145158](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145158):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format strings
- **[gh-144986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144986):** Fix memory leak in `atexit.register()`
- **[gh-88091](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/88091):** Fix `unicodedata.decomposition` for Hangul characters
- **[gh-144782](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144782):** Fix `argparse.ArgumentParser` to be pickleable
- **[gh-144259](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144259):** Fix inconsistent display of long multiline pasted content in the REPL
- **[gh-144156](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144156):** Fix email header folding with RFC 2047 encoded words
- **[gh-66305](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/66305):** Fix hang on Windows in `tempfile` when directory is non-writable
- **[gh-140814](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140814):** `multiprocessing.freeze_support()` no longer sets default start method as side effect
- **[gh-144475](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144475):** Make `repr()` on `functools.partial` safer during concurrent attribute replacement
- **[gh-144538](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144538):** Bump bundled pip in `ensurepip` to 26.0.1
- **[gh-144316](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144316):** Fix crash in `_remote_debugging`
- **[gh-143698](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143698):** Allow `scheduler` and `setpgroup` to be `None` in `os.posix_spawn`; raise `TypeError` instead of `SystemError`
- **[gh-142516](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142516):** Fix `ssl.SSLContext` reference leaks
- **[gh-143304](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143304):** Fix `ctypes.CDLL` to honor the `handle` parameter on POSIX
- **[gh-142763](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142763):** Fix race condition between `ZoneInfo` creation and `clear_cache()`
- **[gh-142352](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142352):** Fix `asyncio.StreamWriter.start_tls` data loss when upgrading to TLS mid-stream
- **[gh-141707](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141707):** Don't change `TarInfo` type from `AREGTYPE` to `DIRTYPE` when parsing GNU long name headers
- **[gh-139933](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139933):** Improve `AttributeError` suggestions for classes with custom `__dir__`
- **[gh-137335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137335):** Eliminate name conflicts for named pipes in `multiprocessing` and `asyncio` on Windows
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** Support lookup for Tangut Ideographs in `unicodedata`

## 4. Documentation

- **[gh-126676](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/126676):** Expand `argparse` documentation for `type=bool` with demonstration of surprising behavior
- **[gh-145649](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145649):** Fix text wrapping in `-X` option descriptions in the `python(1)` man page
- **[gh-145450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145450):** Document missing public `wave.Wave_write` getter methods
- **[gh-136246](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/136246):** New "Improve this page" link in docs sidebar

## 5. Build

- **[gh-146541](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146541):** Android testbed can now be built for 32-bit ARM and x86 targets
- **[gh-146498](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146498):** iOS XCframework build: ensure libpython isn't included in installed app content
- **[gh-146450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146450):** Android build script parity improvements
- **[gh-146446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146446):** iOS XCframework clean target is now more selective for single architectures
- **[gh-145801](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145801):** Use `-fprofile-update=atomic` with GCC PGO to prevent `.gcda` corruption

## 6. Windows

- **[gh-145307](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145307):** Defer loading of `psapi.dll` until used by `ctypes.util.dllist`
- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update bundled OpenSSL to 3.0.19
- **[gh-140131](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140131):** Fix REPL cursor position when module completion suggestion hits console width

## 7. macOS

- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update macOS installer to use OpenSSL 3.0.19
- **[gh-137586](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137586):** Invoke `osascript` with absolute path in `webbrowser` and `turtledemo`

## 8. C API

- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteRepr` now supports `NULL` argument
- **[gh-145010](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145010):** Use GCC dialect alternatives for inline assembly in `object.h` for `-masm=intel` compatibility
- **[gh-144981](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144981):** Make `PyUnstable_Code_SetExtra`, `PyUnstable_Code_GetExtra`, and `PyUnstable_Eval_RequestCodeExtraIndex` thread-safe on free-threaded builds

## 9. Tests

- **[gh-144418](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144418):** Android testbed emulator RAM increased from 2 GB to 4 GB
- **[gh-146202](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146202):** Fix race condition in regrtest temp directory creation on Windows
- **[gh-144739](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144739):** Skip `MemoryProtectionTest` when compiled with older expat but tests run with newer

*Source: [Python 3.14.4 Release](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3144/) · [Full Changelog](https://docs.python.org/release/3.14.4/whatsnew/changelog.html)*

items.title: [python] Bump Python to 3.14.4
items.body:
## 0. Summary of Changes

**Release date:** April 7, 2026
**Scope:** ~337 bugfixes, build improvements, and documentation changes

## 1. Security

| CVE | Issue | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| CVE-2026-4224 | [gh-145986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145986) | Fix crash from unbounded C recursion in `xml.parsers.expat` when converting deeply nested XML content models with `ElementDeclHandler` |
| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
| — | [gh-144370](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144370) | Disallow control characters in status in `wsgiref.handlers` to prevent HTTP header injection |
| — | [gh-143930](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143930) | Reject leading dashes in URLs passed to `webbrowser.open()` |

## 2. Core and Builtins

### 2-1. Crash / Memory Safety Fixes

- **[gh-148157](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148157):** Fix crash when parsing invalid type comments for function parameters (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146615):** Fix crash in `__get__` for `METH_METHOD` descriptors with invalid second argument
- **[gh-145783](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145783):** Fix unlikely parser crash when errors were not propagated (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145792](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145792):** Fix out-of-bounds access invoking faulthandler without VLA support
- **[gh-145701](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145701):** Fix `SystemError` when `__classdict__` or `__conditional_annotations__` is in a class-scope inlined comprehension (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145335):** Fix crash in `os.pathconf` when called with `-1`
- **[gh-145234](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145234):** Fix `SystemError` in parser when encoding cookie decodes to carriage returns
- **[gh-145142](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145142):** Fix crash in free-threaded build when `str.maketrans` dict is concurrently modified
- **[gh-144872](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144872):** Fix heap buffer overflow in the parser (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-144766](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144766):** Fix crash in fork child process when perf support is enabled
- **[gh-144759](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144759):** Fix undefined behavior in lexer with `NULL` pointer arithmetic
- **[gh-144601](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144601):** Fix crash when importing a module whose `PyInit` function raises an exception from a subinterpreter
- **[gh-143636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143636):** Fix crash when calling `SimpleNamespace.__replace__()` on non-namespace instances
- **[gh-140594](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140594):** Fix out-of-bounds read when a single NUL character is read from stdin
- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** Fix `repr()` for lists and tuples containing `NULL`s

### 2-2. Free-Threaded Build Improvements

- **[gh-148144](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148144):** Initialize `_PyInterpreterFrame.visited` when copying frames so incremental GC doesn't read uninitialized bytes
- **[gh-146041](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146041):** Fix scaling bottleneck in `sys.intern` and `PyObject_SetAttr` by avoiding interpreter-wide lock
- **[gh-145779](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145779):** Improve scaling of `classmethod` and `staticmethod` calls
- **[gh-145685](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145685):** Improve scaling of type attribute lookups by avoiding contention on internal type lock
- **[gh-145713](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145713):** Make `bytearray.resize` thread-safe using critical sections
- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects
- **[gh-145036](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145036):** Fix race condition in `list.__sizeof__`
- **[gh-144513](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144513):** Fix potential deadlock using critical sections during stop-the-world pauses
- **[gh-144446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144446):** Fix data races when reading frame object attributes while another thread executes the frame
- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code
- **[gh-146250](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146250):** Fix memory leak in `SyntaxError` when re-initializing
- **[gh-146245](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146245):** Fix reference leaks in `socket` when audit hooks raise exceptions
- **[gh-146196](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146196):** Fix potential UB in `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteASCII` by adding zero-length check
- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios
- **[gh-142183](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142183):** Avoid pathological slowdown from repeated calls at a specific stack depth
- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block
- **[gh-144563](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144563):** Fix Tachyon profiler interaction with `ctypes` and modules loading Python shared library
- **[gh-130555](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130555):** Fix use-after-free in `dict.clear` with embedded values and re-entrant mutation
- **[gh-130327](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130327):** Fix erroneous clearing of `__dict__` if overwritten at runtime
- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure
- **[gh-144833](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144833):** Fix use-after-free in `ssl` when `SSL_new()` returns `NULL`
- **[gh-143637](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143637):** Fix crash in `socket.sendmsg()` from re-entrant ancillary data mutation
- **[gh-143543](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143543):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` from re-entrant `__eq__` during key comparison
- **[gh-140652](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140652):** Fix crash in `_interpchannels.list_all` after closing a channel
- **[gh-142781](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142781):** Fix `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo` crash when internal class-level cache is inconsistent
- **[gh-142787](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142787):** Fix `sqlite3` assertion failure in blob subscript with empty slices

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

- **[gh-145883](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145883):** Fix heap buffer overflow reads from malformed TZif data in `zoneinfo` (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146310](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146310):** `ensurepip` no longer looks for `pip-*.whl` in the current directory
- **[gh-146083](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146083):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.5
- **[gh-144363](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144363):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.4

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-144777](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144777):** Fix data races in `io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder`
- **[gh-144809](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144809):** Make `collections.deque` copy atomic
- **[gh-144494](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144494):** Fix performance regression in `asyncio.all_tasks`
- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-146556](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146556):** Fix `annotationlib.get_annotations` hanging on circular `__wrapped__` chains
- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`
- **[gh-146076](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146076):** Fix `zoneinfo` crashes when deleting `_weak_cache` from a subclass
- **[gh-146054](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146054):** Limit size of `encodings.search_function` cache (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146004](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146004):** All `-X` options now propagated to child processes spawned by `multiprocessing`
- **[gh-145754](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145754):** Fix mock autospec with `FORWARDREF` annotation format
- **[gh-145750](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145750):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format string parsing (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145492](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145492):** Fix infinite recursion in `collections.defaultdict.__repr__` when self-referencing
- **[gh-145616](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145616):** Detect Android sysconfig ABI correctly on 32-bit ARM
- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)
- **[gh-145158](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145158):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format strings
- **[gh-144986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144986):** Fix memory leak in `atexit.register()`
- **[gh-88091](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/88091):** Fix `unicodedata.decomposition` for Hangul characters
- **[gh-144782](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144782):** Fix `argparse.ArgumentParser` to be pickleable
- **[gh-144259](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144259):** Fix inconsistent display of long multiline pasted content in the REPL
- **[gh-144156](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144156):** Fix email header folding with RFC 2047 encoded words
- **[gh-66305](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/66305):** Fix hang on Windows in `tempfile` when directory is non-writable
- **[gh-140814](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140814):** `multiprocessing.freeze_support()` no longer sets default start method as side effect
- **[gh-144475](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144475):** Make `repr()` on `functools.partial` safer during concurrent attribute replacement
- **[gh-144538](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144538):** Bump bundled pip in `ensurepip` to 26.0.1
- **[gh-144316](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144316):** Fix crash in `_remote_debugging`
- **[gh-143698](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143698):** Allow `scheduler` and `setpgroup` to be `None` in `os.posix_spawn`; raise `TypeError` instead of `SystemError`
- **[gh-142516](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142516):** Fix `ssl.SSLContext` reference leaks
- **[gh-143304](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143304):** Fix `ctypes.CDLL` to honor the `handle` parameter on POSIX
- **[gh-142763](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142763):** Fix race condition between `ZoneInfo` creation and `clear_cache()`
- **[gh-142352](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142352):** Fix `asyncio.StreamWriter.start_tls` data loss when upgrading to TLS mid-stream
- **[gh-141707](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141707):** Don't change `TarInfo` type from `AREGTYPE` to `DIRTYPE` when parsing GNU long name headers
- **[gh-139933](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139933):** Improve `AttributeError` suggestions for classes with custom `__dir__`
- **[gh-137335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137335):** Eliminate name conflicts for named pipes in `multiprocessing` and `asyncio` on Windows
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** Support lookup for Tangut Ideographs in `unicodedata`

## 4. Documentation

- **[gh-126676](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/126676):** Expand `argparse` documentation for `type=bool` with demonstration of surprising behavior
- **[gh-145649](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145649):** Fix text wrapping in `-X` option descriptions in the `python(1)` man page
- **[gh-145450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145450):** Document missing public `wave.Wave_write` getter methods
- **[gh-136246](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/136246):** New "Improve this page" link in docs sidebar

## 5. Build

- **[gh-146541](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146541):** Android testbed can now be built for 32-bit ARM and x86 targets
- **[gh-146498](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146498):** iOS XCframework build: ensure libpython isn't included in installed app content
- **[gh-146450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146450):** Android build script parity improvements
- **[gh-146446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146446):** iOS XCframework clean target is now more selective for single architectures
- **[gh-145801](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145801):** Use `-fprofile-update=atomic` with GCC PGO to prevent `.gcda` corruption

## 6. Windows

- **[gh-145307](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145307):** Defer loading of `psapi.dll` until used by `ctypes.util.dllist`
- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update bundled OpenSSL to 3.0.19
- **[gh-140131](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140131):** Fix REPL cursor position when module completion suggestion hits console width

## 7. macOS

- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update macOS installer to use OpenSSL 3.0.19
- **[gh-137586](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137586):** Invoke `osascript` with absolute path in `webbrowser` and `turtledemo`

## 8. C API

- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteRepr` now supports `NULL` argument
- **[gh-145010](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145010):** Use GCC dialect alternatives for inline assembly in `object.h` for `-masm=intel` compatibility
- **[gh-144981](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144981):** Make `PyUnstable_Code_SetExtra`, `PyUnstable_Code_GetExtra`, and `PyUnstable_Eval_RequestCodeExtraIndex` thread-safe on free-threaded builds

## 9. Tests

- **[gh-144418](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144418):** Android testbed emulator RAM increased from 2 GB to 4 GB
- **[gh-146202](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146202):** Fix race condition in regrtest temp directory creation on Windows
- **[gh-144739](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144739):** Skip `MemoryProtectionTest` when compiled with older expat but tests run with newer

*Source: [Python 3.14.4 Release](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3144/) · [Full Changelog](https://docs.python.org/release/3.14.4/whatsnew/changelog.html)*

items.title: refactor: consolidate duplicate Python helpers into pkg/tools/python/
items.body:
## What Changed

Five Python venv/pip orchestration helpers duplicated between the hook executor (python_executor.go) and the framework service ( ramework_service_python.go) are now shared through pkg/tools/python/. Both consumers already depended on this package, so no new dependency edges were introduced. This eliminates ~80-100 lines of duplication and ensures future changes only need to happen in one place.

## How It Works

Extracted shared logic into exported functions/methods on python.Cli: VenvNameForDir() for naming, EnsureVirtualEnv() for stat→create, InstallDependencies() for pip/pyproject dispatch, ResolveCommand() for py/python/python3 resolution, and VenvPythonPath()/VenvActivateCmd() for platform-aware venv paths. The executor's stricter implementation (with rrors.Is and directory type checks) was used as the canonical version. The pythonTools interface was simplified accordingly.

## Issue References

Fixes #7570
Relates to #7435

items.title: Declare free-threaded Python support on the python_function plugin.
items.body:
<!---
Thank you for contributing to NVIDIA DALI! If you haven't yet,
please read the contributing guidelines in the CONTRIBUTING.md file.

We need a few more information from you to proceed.
Please fill the relevant sections in this PR template.

Fields in the Checklist section can be marked after you create and save the Pull Request.
--->


## Category:

**Other** (*e.g. Documentation, Tests, Configuration*)


## Description:

The DALI backend Pybind11 extension already declares support for free-threaded Python. This PR does the same for `python_function_plugin`. This is the missing piece for full free-threaded Python support.

Since `python_function_plugin` is always transitively imported, not declaring free-threaded support results in the GIL always being re-enabled for DALI:

items.title: Create complete MusicManager project (Python backend + C# WPF GUI)
items.body:
Bootstraps the MusicManager project from an empty repo — a full-stack music library manager with a Python backend exposing a JSON CLI and a WPF frontend consuming it via MVVM.

## Backend (`Backend/`)
- **`main.py`** — entry point; parses args, inits DB/config, routes to modules, all output as JSON to stdout
- **`modules/config.py`** — YAML config singleton; generates `config.yaml` with sane defaults on first run
- **`modules/database.py`** — peewee ORM over SQLite; tables: `tracks`, `artists`, `albums`, `playlists`, `playlist_tracks`, `queue`, `sync_log`
- **`modules/downloader.py`** — spotDL wrapper; downloads to `_Temp/` first; `ThreadPoolExecutor` (max 2); retry ×3 with 300 s backoff
- **`modules/tagger.py`** — enriches tags via MusicBrainz / Last.fm / Genius; embeds lyrics; writes ReplayGain via ffmpeg; backs up original tags before mutation
- **`modules/organizer.py`** — builds library folder structure from config templates; Windows filename sanitization (`<>:"/\|?*`); MD5-based dedup; 180-char max path segment
- **`modules/folder_art.py`** — cover art pipeline (MusicBrainz min 1000 px → iTunes → Spotify); writes `folder.jpg` + `desktop.ini` with correct Hidden/System/ReadOnly attributes
- **`modules/instrumental.py`** — searches Spotify/YouTube for instrumental versions; ±10 s duration tolerance; organizes under `Strumentali/{Album} [Instrumental]/`
- **`modules/playlist.py`** — full playlist CRUD; copies files with `{NN}. {Artist} - {Title}.flac` numbering; maintains `playlist.json` manifest; syncs tags between `Artisti/` and `Playlist/` copies
- **`modules/inbox_watcher.py`** — watchdog on `_Inbox/`; 3 s debounce; subfolder name → playlist destination; clears subfolder after processing
- **`modules/queue_watcher.py`** — parses `queue.txt` (URLs or plain names); writes back `[✅ date]` / `[❌]` status markers
- **`modules/device.py`** — detects TS1802 SD card; FLAC → MP3 320 kbps via ffmpeg; incremental sync (new / updated / removed); reports all four categories
- **`modules/sync.py`** — reads `.spotdl` files from `data/sync/`; diffs against current Spotify playlist state; downloads additions, removes deletions, renumbers on reorder
- **`modules/foobar.py`** — triggers foobar2000 library refresh via its CLI
- **`modules/cli.py`** — `--download`, `--queue`, `--sync`, `--check`, `--fix`, `--stats`, `--search`, `--export-device`, `--retag`, `--playlist-{list,add,remove,reorder}`, `--status`
- **`sync-all.bat` / `sync-playlist.bat` / `export-device.bat`** — convenience wrappers

## GUI (`GUI/`) — C# WPF, net8.0-windows, MVVM
- **Dark theme** defined in `App.xaml` (`#1E1E1E` bg, `#0078D4` accent); applied globally to Button, TextBox, ComboBox, ListView, TabItem
- **`MainWindow`** — 3-tab shell (Download / Playlist / Dispositivo) with bottom status bar
- **`DownloadTab`** — URL/name input; queue `ListView` with status emoji (✅ ⏳ 🕐 ❌), per-item progress bar, Retry button
- **`PlaylistTab`** — playlist selector; drag-drop zone (`AllowDrop=True`, mixed file+folder drops); track list with position, reorder arrows, remove button
- **`DeviceTab`** — device label (`💾 D:\ (TS1802) — 4.2 GB liberi`); playlist checklist with track count + estimated MB; per-playlist export progress
- **`PythonBridge`** — launches `python main.py <args>` as async `Process`; streams JSON stdout line-by-line; exposes `OutputReceived` event
- **`DeviceMonitor`** — WMI `ManagementEventWatcher` for USB insertion/removal; filters by "TS1802" volume label; raises typed `DeviceConnected` / `DeviceDisconnected` events
- **`FileDropHandler`** — static `HandleDrop(DragEventArgs)`; top-level audio file extraction from dropped files/folders; filters `.flac .mp3 .m4a .opus .ogg .wav`

## Config & housekeeping
- `config.yaml` (gitignored) generated on first run with full path/audio/metadata/naming/device/API-key template
- `.gitignore` excludes `config.yaml`, `data/music.db`, `data/music.log`, `data/sync/`, `__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `bin/`, `obj/`

items.title: Rename Python bindings and C++ class from `Coda*` prefix to unprefixed names
items.body:
All Python binding classes and exceptions carried a redundant `Coda` prefix (e.g. `CodaDoc`, `CodaBlock`, `CodaException`). Since the module is already named `coda`, the prefix is noise. This PR strips it so the API reads naturally as `coda.Doc`, `coda.Block`, etc.

The C++ top-level class was similarly renamed from `Coda` to `coda::Doc` to match the Python naming and better reflect what the object represents.

### Python (`bindings/python/`)
- `CodaDoc` → `Doc`, `CodaBlock` → `Block`, `CodaArray` → `Array`, `CodaTable` → `Table`, `CodaKeyedTable` → `KeyedTable`, `CodaRow` → `Row`, `CodaString` → `String`
- `CodaException` → `Error`, `CodaParseError` → `ParseError`
- Updated `coda.py`, `coda.pyi`, `__init__.py` — all internal references, docstrings, type annotations, and `__all__` exports

### C++ (`include/coda.hpp`, `src/coda.hpp`)
- `Coda` class renamed to `coda::Doc`

### Docs & examples
- `docs/API-CPP.md`, `docs/API-PYTHON.md`, `examples/cpp.cpp`, `examples/python.py`, `README.md` updated to reflect new names

**Before:**
```python
from coda_format import CodaDoc
with CodaDoc.parse_file("config.coda") as doc:
    print(doc.root()["key"])
```

**After:**
```python
import coda
with coda.Doc.parse_file("config.coda") as doc:
    print(doc.root()["key"])
```

items.title: [python] Bump Python to 3.14.4
items.body:
## 0. Summary of Changes

**Release date:** April 7, 2026
**Scope:** ~337 bugfixes, build improvements, and documentation changes

## 1. Security

| CVE | Issue | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| CVE-2026-4224 | [gh-145986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145986) | Fix crash from unbounded C recursion in `xml.parsers.expat` when converting deeply nested XML content models with `ElementDeclHandler` |
| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
| — | [gh-144370](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144370) | Disallow control characters in status in `wsgiref.handlers` to prevent HTTP header injection |
| — | [gh-143930](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143930) | Reject leading dashes in URLs passed to `webbrowser.open()` |

## 2. Core and Builtins

### 2-1. Crash / Memory Safety Fixes

- **[gh-148157](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148157):** Fix crash when parsing invalid type comments for function parameters (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146615):** Fix crash in `__get__` for `METH_METHOD` descriptors with invalid second argument
- **[gh-145783](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145783):** Fix unlikely parser crash when errors were not propagated (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145792](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145792):** Fix out-of-bounds access invoking faulthandler without VLA support
- **[gh-145701](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145701):** Fix `SystemError` when `__classdict__` or `__conditional_annotations__` is in a class-scope inlined comprehension (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145335):** Fix crash in `os.pathconf` when called with `-1`
- **[gh-145234](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145234):** Fix `SystemError` in parser when encoding cookie decodes to carriage returns
- **[gh-145142](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145142):** Fix crash in free-threaded build when `str.maketrans` dict is concurrently modified
- **[gh-144872](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144872):** Fix heap buffer overflow in the parser (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-144766](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144766):** Fix crash in fork child process when perf support is enabled
- **[gh-144759](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144759):** Fix undefined behavior in lexer with `NULL` pointer arithmetic
- **[gh-144601](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144601):** Fix crash when importing a module whose `PyInit` function raises an exception from a subinterpreter
- **[gh-143636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143636):** Fix crash when calling `SimpleNamespace.__replace__()` on non-namespace instances
- **[gh-140594](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140594):** Fix out-of-bounds read when a single NUL character is read from stdin
- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** Fix `repr()` for lists and tuples containing `NULL`s

### 2-2. Free-Threaded Build Improvements

- **[gh-148144](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148144):** Initialize `_PyInterpreterFrame.visited` when copying frames so incremental GC doesn't read uninitialized bytes
- **[gh-146041](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146041):** Fix scaling bottleneck in `sys.intern` and `PyObject_SetAttr` by avoiding interpreter-wide lock
- **[gh-145779](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145779):** Improve scaling of `classmethod` and `staticmethod` calls
- **[gh-145685](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145685):** Improve scaling of type attribute lookups by avoiding contention on internal type lock
- **[gh-145713](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145713):** Make `bytearray.resize` thread-safe using critical sections
- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects
- **[gh-145036](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145036):** Fix race condition in `list.__sizeof__`
- **[gh-144513](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144513):** Fix potential deadlock using critical sections during stop-the-world pauses
- **[gh-144446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144446):** Fix data races when reading frame object attributes while another thread executes the frame
- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code
- **[gh-146250](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146250):** Fix memory leak in `SyntaxError` when re-initializing
- **[gh-146245](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146245):** Fix reference leaks in `socket` when audit hooks raise exceptions
- **[gh-146196](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146196):** Fix potential UB in `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteASCII` by adding zero-length check
- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios
- **[gh-142183](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142183):** Avoid pathological slowdown from repeated calls at a specific stack depth
- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block
- **[gh-144563](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144563):** Fix Tachyon profiler interaction with `ctypes` and modules loading Python shared library
- **[gh-130555](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130555):** Fix use-after-free in `dict.clear` with embedded values and re-entrant mutation
- **[gh-130327](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130327):** Fix erroneous clearing of `__dict__` if overwritten at runtime
- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure

items.title: Convert all Python solutions to C++
Remove `CodaDoc.file()` and `CodaFile` from Python bindings to match C++ API
items.body:
Python bindings had a stale `file()` alias and `CodaFile` stub that no longer exist in the C++ API after the root-access cleanup. This aligns the Python surface with C++, where `Coda::operator[]` and the file layer were removed in favour of explicit `root()` access.

## Changes

- **`coda.py`**: Removed `CodaDoc.file()` backward-compat alias for `root()`
- **`coda.pyi`**: Removed `CodaFile` class and `CodaDoc.file()` stub; added `CodaDoc.root() -> CodaBlock` stub

## API after this change

```python
# Before (no longer valid)
doc.file()["key"]

# Correct usage
doc.root()["key"]
```

`CodaDoc` has no `__getitem__`/`__setitem__`, so direct `doc["key"]` was never permitted. `CodaTestRunner` already used `doc.root()` — no test changes required.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

> 2.  **Ensure uniformity with C++**: In C++, the `Coda` class was updated to remove `operator[]` and expose `root()`/`root() const` instead. The Python `CodaDoc` class already has a `root()` method, but it should be checked to ensure it doesn't have `__getitem__` or `__setitem__` overloads that bypass the root block.
> 3.  **Clean up naming**: The `CodaDoc.file()` method was kept as a backward-compat alias in a previous PR but should probably be removed or discouraged if we are fully moving to the `root()` terminology.
> 4.  **Update Tests**: Update the `CodaTestRunner` and any other tests in `bindings/python/coda.py` to use `doc.root()["key"]` instead of `doc["key"]` (if any such usage exists).
> 
> Review `bindings/python/coda.py` and ensure it behaves exactly like the new C++ `Coda` class regarding root access.

> Note: In the current `bindings/python/coda.py`, `CodaDoc` does not appear to have `__getitem__`, but the `CodaTestRunner` uses `self.root = doc.root()` and then accesses `self.root[field]`. However, some logic might still refer to the old `file()` alias or might have been intended to be updated.

items.title: feat: c like lang

## Summary

Adds **C, Go, and Java** as first-class `sugar-high/presets`, introduces optional **`typeKeywords`** so built-in types use the existing `class` token (distinct from `keyword`), expands docs/tests, and trims the homepage carousel so non-web samples live next to the preset examples.

## Core

- **`HighlightOptions.typeKeywords`**: optional `Set<string>`; matched before `keywords`, emitted as the `class` token type (same slot themes already use for type-like highlighting).

## Presets (`sugar-high/presets`)

- New: **`c`**, **`go`**, **`java`** with shared **`clike-base`** comment rules (`//`, `/* */`).
- **`typeKeywords`** for built-in / primitive-style tokens; remaining language words stay in **`keywords`**.

## Docs

- **Carousel**: removed long Python / C / Java cards; keeps web-oriented stack (Rust, CSS, JS literals, TS).
- **Install banner**: preset mapping example, **C / Java / Python** sample blocks with comments and spacing; samples use **`highlight(code, preset)` + Codice `asMarkup`** so highlighting matches sugar-high (Codice’s built-in extension map only wires css/py/rs today).
- **`syntax-highlight-presets`**: adds `c`, `go`, `java` for the live editor extension picker where applicable.

## Tests

- Preset tests for **c**, **go**, **java**; **css** regression; **`typeKeywords`** unit test in `tokenize.test.ts`.

## Remark plugin

- Unchanged behavior: fence language must match an **exported preset name** exactly (e.g. ` ```c ` → `presets.c`).

items.title: Improve performance in jitcdde_template.c
items.body:
This has two performance improvements:
1. Uses Horner to evaluate some of the cubic polynomials. As far as I can tell, this has a very negligible effect (likely because `-O3` does it anyway).
2. Passes anchors around by pointer instead of by value. This seems to have a noticeable effect for larger systems, because it avoids a bunch of copies.

I've tested the changes on the `examples/kuramoto_network.py` example (using hyperfine to do full timing) and using [this benchmark](https://gist.github.com/alexfikl/c9864709490653b6f3096731cdf16e80) (to time separate parts). The results can be seen below.

This was run on my machine (AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 370) and Python 3.14 using GCC and SymEngine 0.14.0. The huge amount of time is spent in the compilation step, but not sure we can do much there (between symengine and gcc doing work). The improvement I was hoping for is in the `run` part, which seems pretty nice :grin:

What do you think? Also checked that there are no new leaks reported by valgrind.

#### Before
```
> hyperfine 'python -O examples/kuramoto_network.py' 
Benchmark 1: python -O examples/kuramoto_network.py
  Time (mean ± σ):      2.774 s ±  0.036 s    [User: 2.765 s, System: 0.121 s]
  Range (min … max):    2.725 s …  2.827 s    10 runs
```
```
> python -O benchmark_kuramoto.py --reps 10 --n 200    
Results (omp=off)  (n=200, 10 reps, mean ± stdev)
  setup        1.1 ms  ±  1.3 ms
  compile      4.806 s  ±  228.4 ms
  transient    2.5 ms  ±  1.8 ms
  run          201.5 ms  ±  84.6 ms
  total        5.011 s  ±  184.9 ms
```

#### After
```
> hyperfine 'python -O examples/kuramoto_network.py'
Benchmark 1: python examples/kuramoto_network.py
  Time (mean ± σ):      1.679 s ±  0.067 s    [User: 1.568 s, System: 0.109 s]
  Range (min … max):    1.626 s …  1.858 s    10 runs
```
```
> python -O benchmark_kuramoto.py --reps 10 --n 200    
Results (n=200, 10 reps, mean ± stdev)
  setup        1.1 ms  ±  1.3 ms
  compile      4.679 s  ±  178.8 ms
  transient    1.8 ms  ±  1.4 ms
  run          123.2 ms  ±  52.1 ms
  total        4.805 s  ±  150.8 ms
```

I'm not sure why the `hyperfine` run was so much faster after, since it really should be dominated by the compilation part.

#### Wilson-Cowan

I've also tested this on my own code (a similarly sized Wilson-Cowan system with fewer delays) and it seems to improve the situation quite a lot. When running a Lyapunov exponent calculation, I'm seeing
```
# before 
eps = 0.000 tau = 1.610 behavior 'Periodic' (wall 50.14251s)
# after
eps = 0.000 tau = 1.610 behavior 'Periodic' (wall 5.90889s)
```

This is a bit dramatic and I'm not quite sure why it improves it so much, but it seems repeatable with the changes on this branch. I can make an smaller script for it, if you think it's useful.

items.title: Convert CS C# scripts to GDScript equivalents
items.body:
### Motivation
- Provide GDScript versions of all gameplay and utility scripts contained in the `CS/` folder so the project can run or be inspected in a Godot/GDScript context. 
- Preserve original runtime logic and data without changing scene wiring or altering the existing `.cs` sources. 
- Keep conversion strictly to language translation and data migration (not refactors or feature changes).

### Description
- Added GDScript equivalents for each `.cs` file under `CS/`: `PRNG.gd`, `SpaceChunk.gd`, `HelpUI.gd`, `PlanetUIElement.gd`, `StarUI.gd`, `SpaceSceneManager.gd`, `Naming.gd`, and `Star.gd`, with `class_name` and `@export` bindings mirroring original exports. 
- Ported PRNG methods to `PRNG.gd` (`seed_with_u64`, `seed_with_string`, `next_int`, `next_float`) while keeping seeded behavior compatible with Godot's `RandomNumberGenerator`. 
- Migrated UI and gameplay logic (input/event handling, chunk instantiation, star/planet generation) into GDScript preserving method names and behaviors (for example `generate`, `_input_event`, `populate_star_info`, `set_coords`, `is_ui_open`). 
- Migrated the large name/wordlists and `generate_name` logic into `Naming.gd` (the long arrays from `Naming.cs` were preserved and translated to GDScript arrays). 

### Testing
- Verified that every `.cs` filename in `CS/` has a corresponding `.gd` file using a `python` check that compared stems (`python` script succeeded and reported no missing translations). 
- Confirmed new files are present with `git status --short` and committed the conversion with `git commit -m "Convert CS folder scripts to GDScript"`, both commands completed successfully. 
- Ran repository listings (`rg`/`find`) during the process to ensure files were created under `CS/` and the conversion produced the expected output files, which succeeded.

items.title: feat: introduce native Python asyncio support
items.body:
## Summary
This PR introduces native Python `asyncio` integration for the `unilink` library. It provides a pure Python wrapper layer that bridges the C++ asynchronous core with the Python event loop, allowing developers to use modern `async/await` syntax for all core transport protocols.

## Changes
- **Python `unilink.asyncio` Package**:
  - Created a new subpackage providing `AsyncTcpClient`, `AsyncUdsClient`, `AsyncUdp`, and `AsyncSerial`.
  - Implemented a thread-safe bridge using `loop.call_soon_threadsafe` to pipe C++ callbacks into `asyncio.Queue`.
  - Added support for asynchronous message framing (Line/Packet) within the `asyncio` wrappers.
- **C++ Transport Enhancements (UDP)**:
  - Fixed a critical blocking issue in `UdpChannel::start()` where it would hang waiting for a state transition that never occurred in connectionless mode.
  - Improved `UdpChannel` to allow immediate data transmission if a remote address is configured, removing the previous restriction of needing to receive a packet first.
  - Refactored `std::promise` handling in the UDP wrapper to prevent potential `std::future_error` or memory corruption in multi-threaded Python environments.
- **Python Binding Improvements**:
  - Ensured proper object ownership in the `asyncio` layer to prevent premature garbage collection of C++ wrapper instances.
- **Documentation**:
  - Added an `asyncio` usage section to the Python Bindings guide (planned/implied by the new package structure).

## Related Issues
- Fixes # (Requirement for modern Python async/await support)

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [x] Documentation update
- [x] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] Performance improvement

## Additional Notes
While TCP and UDS async clients are fully verified and performant, UDP receiving currently faces a bottleneck due to the high-frequency interaction between C++ threads and the Python GIL. This remains a known area for future performance optimization, though the reliability of UDP transmission and initialization has been significantly improved.

items.title: [python] Bump Python to 3.14.4
items.body:
## 0. Summary of Changes

**Release date:** April 7, 2026
**Scope:** ~337 bugfixes, build improvements, and documentation changes

## 1. Security

| CVE | Issue | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| CVE-2026-4224 | [gh-145986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145986) | Fix crash from unbounded C recursion in `xml.parsers.expat` when converting deeply nested XML content models with `ElementDeclHandler` |
| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
| — | [gh-144370](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144370) | Disallow control characters in status in `wsgiref.handlers` to prevent HTTP header injection |
| — | [gh-143930](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143930) | Reject leading dashes in URLs passed to `webbrowser.open()` |

## 2. Core and Builtins

### 2-1. Crash / Memory Safety Fixes

- **[gh-148157](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148157):** Fix crash when parsing invalid type comments for function parameters (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146615):** Fix crash in `__get__` for `METH_METHOD` descriptors with invalid second argument
- **[gh-145783](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145783):** Fix unlikely parser crash when errors were not propagated (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145792](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145792):** Fix out-of-bounds access invoking faulthandler without VLA support
- **[gh-145701](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145701):** Fix `SystemError` when `__classdict__` or `__conditional_annotations__` is in a class-scope inlined comprehension (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145335):** Fix crash in `os.pathconf` when called with `-1`
- **[gh-145234](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145234):** Fix `SystemError` in parser when encoding cookie decodes to carriage returns
- **[gh-145142](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145142):** Fix crash in free-threaded build when `str.maketrans` dict is concurrently modified
- **[gh-144872](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144872):** Fix heap buffer overflow in the parser (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-144766](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144766):** Fix crash in fork child process when perf support is enabled
- **[gh-144759](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144759):** Fix undefined behavior in lexer with `NULL` pointer arithmetic
- **[gh-144601](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144601):** Fix crash when importing a module whose `PyInit` function raises an exception from a subinterpreter
- **[gh-143636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143636):** Fix crash when calling `SimpleNamespace.__replace__()` on non-namespace instances
- **[gh-140594](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140594):** Fix out-of-bounds read when a single NUL character is read from stdin
- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** Fix `repr()` for lists and tuples containing `NULL`s

### 2-2. Free-Threaded Build Improvements

- **[gh-148144](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148144):** Initialize `_PyInterpreterFrame.visited` when copying frames so incremental GC doesn't read uninitialized bytes
- **[gh-146041](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146041):** Fix scaling bottleneck in `sys.intern` and `PyObject_SetAttr` by avoiding interpreter-wide lock
- **[gh-145779](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145779):** Improve scaling of `classmethod` and `staticmethod` calls
- **[gh-145685](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145685):** Improve scaling of type attribute lookups by avoiding contention on internal type lock
- **[gh-145713](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145713):** Make `bytearray.resize` thread-safe using critical sections
- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects

- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code

- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios

- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block

- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`

- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)

items.title: Add C API to retrieve PlayerRound hand
items.body:
### Motivation
- Provide a C ABI to let external callers (C/Python via ctypes) read the current 13-card hand stored in a `PlayerRound` instance.

### Description
- Add `tc_player_round_get_hand(tc_player_round_t player, int out_hand13[13])` declaration to `src/cpp/manager.h` and implement it in `src/cpp/manager.cpp` to forward to the C++ layer via `PlayerRound::getHand`.
- Add `int PlayerRound::getHand(int out_hand13[13]) const` declaration in `src/cpp/players/player.h` and implementation in `src/cpp/players/player.cpp` which copies the internal `Pattern::hand` buffer into the provided caller buffer using `memcpy`.
- The new functions perform basic validation and return error codes: `tc_player_round_get_hand` returns `-1` if `player` or `out_hand13` is null; `PlayerRound::getHand` returns `-1` if `out_hand13` is null and `-2` if the internal hand (`m_pat`) is not initialized.
- Modified files: `src/cpp/manager.h`, `src/cpp/manager.cpp`, `src/cpp/players/player.h`, and `src/cpp/players/player.cpp`.

### Testing
- Ran CMake configure/build via `cmake -S src/cpp -B /tmp/thirteen-build && cmake --build /tmp/thirteen-build -j2`, which failed during generation with `Cannot find source file: src/cpp/manager.cpp` reported by CMake, so a full build could not complete in this environment.

items.title: [ENG-488] Add C/C++ collector + policy
items.body:
## What's included

- `collectors/cpp/lunar-collector.yml` — Plugin manifest with 5 sub-collectors
- `collectors/cpp/README.md` — Documentation
- `collectors/cpp/assets/cpp.svg` — Icon (fill=black, from simple-icons)

## Design summary

Writes to `.lang.cpp` following the established language collector pattern (matches Go, Java, .NET structure).

### Sub-collectors

| Name | Hook | What it does |
|------|------|-------------|
| `project` | code | Detects build systems (CMake, Make, Meson, Autotools, Bazel), source file counts, C++ standard version, package manager config |
| `dependencies` | code | Extracts deps from Conan (`conanfile.txt`/`conanfile.py`), vcpkg (`vcpkg.json`), and CMake `find_package()` |
| `cppcheck` | code | Optional static analysis — skips gracefully if cppcheck not installed |
| `cicd` | ci-before-command | Tracks compiler commands (gcc, g++, clang, clang++) with version |
| `cmake-cicd` | ci-before-command | Tracks cmake commands with version |

### Component JSON paths

- `.lang.cpp` — project metadata, build systems, source file counts, C++ standard
- `.lang.cpp.dependencies` — direct deps + cmake packages
- `.lang.cpp.lint` — normalized cppcheck warnings
- `.lang.cpp.native.cppcheck` — raw cppcheck output
- `.lang.cpp.cicd` — compiler/cmake commands from CI

## Relationship to existing plugins

- Follows the same `.lang.<language>` pattern as Go, Java, .NET, Rust, Python, PHP, Node.js
- No existing C/C++ collector — this is net-new
- No policy included per ticket scope ("Policy can be added later")
- cppcheck sub-collector mirrors the golangci-lint pattern from the Go collector

## Testing plan

- **Project detection:** Test against repos with CMake, Make, and bare C/C++ source files
- **Dependencies:** Test against repos with conanfile.txt, vcpkg.json, and CMakeLists.txt with find_package()
- **cppcheck:** Test with cppcheck installed (expect lint results) and without (expect graceful skip)
- **CI collectors:** Test on cronos with gcc/clang compile steps in GitHub Actions
- **Edge cases:** No C/C++ files (should write nothing), mixed C/C++ and other languages

## Open questions

1. Should `make` commands be tracked in CI? Decided against it since `make` is too generic (used for non-C/C++ too). Open to adding if reviewers disagree.
2. The `base-main` image should suffice for project/dependencies. cppcheck can be installed via `apk add` in the script. Worth creating a dedicated `cpp-main` image instead?

---

items.title: feat: Option C — replace Node server with Vite dev proxy
items.body:
## Summary

Implements Option C from the architecture review: delete the Node/Express backend entirely and replace it with a Vite dev proxy. Python/FastAPI (`backend/server.py`) is now the only API runtime for both dev and production.

**Dev topology:**
```
browser → Vite :5173 (HMR) → /api proxy → Python FastAPI :8000
```
**Prod topology (unchanged):**
```
browser → nginx :80 → /api proxy → Python FastAPI :8000 (internal)
```

## What changed

| | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Dev server | `tsx server/index.ts` (Express + Vite middleware) | `vite` (standalone) |
| Build | `tsx script/build.ts` (esbuild server + vite client) | `vite build` |
| API in dev | Node/Express (MemStorage, demo data) | Python FastAPI (SQLite) |
| API in prod | Python FastAPI | Python FastAPI (unchanged) |

**Deleted:** `server/` (7 files), `script/build.ts`, `drizzle.config.ts`

**Modified:**
- `shared/schema.ts` → plain TS interfaces (no drizzle-orm dependency); client `import type` unchanged
- `vite.config.ts` → `/api` proxy to `localhost:8000`, dropped unused `@assets` alias
- `package.json` → simplified scripts; removed ~15 server-only dependencies (`express`, `passport`, `pg`, `drizzle-*`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `ws`, etc.)
- `tsconfig.json` → removed `server/**/*` from includes, dropped `"node"` type
- `docker-compose.override.yml` → new file; exposes `backend:8000` → `localhost:8000` for local dev

## Local dev workflow after this PR

```bash
# Terminal 1 — start Python backend
docker compose up backend

# Terminal 2 — start Vite with HMR
npm install   # first time after dep cleanup
npm run dev
```

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm run build` produces a clean Vite build with no TS errors
- [ ] `docker compose up --build` starts both services as before
- [ ] `npm run dev` + `docker compose up backend` — all `/api/*` calls proxy correctly to Python
- [ ] Frontend pages (Dashboard, Activities, Trends, Reports, Settings) load and fetch data

feat: implement C#/.NET hook executor
items.body:
## What Changed

azd now supports C#/.NET hook scripts (`.cs` files) alongside the existing Bash, PowerShell, Python, JavaScript, and TypeScript executors. .NET developers — especially those working with Aspire-based projects — can now write hook automation in C# using either a project-backed approach or single-file scripts.

Two execution modes are supported:
- **Project mode**: When a `.csproj` (or `.fsproj`/`.vbproj`) is discovered near the script via walk-up, azd automatically runs `dotnet restore` → `dotnet build` during the Prepare phase, then executes via `dotnet run --project`.
- **Single-file mode**: On .NET 10+, standalone `.cs` files run directly via `dotnet run script.cs` without needing a project file. On older SDKs, a clear error with upgrade guidance is shown.

Auto-detection works out of the box — a hook with `run: ./hooks/seed.cs` automatically selects the .NET executor. Explicit `kind: dotnet` override is also supported.

## How It Works

The implementation follows the same `HookExecutor` Prepare/Execute/Cleanup lifecycle used by all other language executors. A `dotnetTools` interface decouples the executor from the concrete `dotnet.Cli` for testability, matching the `pythonTools` and `nodeTools` patterns. A dedicated `DiscoverDotNetProject()` function (mirroring `DiscoverNodeProject()`) walks up from the script to find `.NET` project files, avoiding false negatives in mixed-language directories where Python or Node.js files would shadow `.csproj` in the generic discovery priority list. SDK version detection via a new `SdkVersion()` method on `dotnet.Cli` gates single-file mode to .NET 10+. Missing SDK errors use `ErrorWithSuggestion` with install links.

## Issue References

Resolves #7623

## Notes

- F#/VB.NET hook scripts are not explicitly targeted but `.fsproj`/`.vbproj` project discovery works via the existing `*.*proj` glob pattern.
- Single-file mode requires .NET SDK 10.0.0+. Users on older SDKs receive a clear error suggesting they create a `.csproj` or upgrade.
